A plan may otherwise remain in the task's coordination state or in the user-requested artifact.\n\n## Elapsed-time planning priority\n\nCorrectness, completeness, user intent, safety, required evidence, ownership discipline, and validation quality are hard constraints. Within those constraints, every plan must organize scheduling, delegation, worker capability, integration, and validation to produce the correct integrated result in the minimum practical elapsed time. A distinct verification lane may still be useful.\n\n### Tier 1: standard multi-step task\n\nUse Tier 1 for medium work. Record the objective; scope and exclusions; governing sources; repository/working-tree reality; assumptions and unknowns; compact dependency graph and parallelization assessment; critical path and elapsed-time strategy; lane matrix; subagent communication plan; file and shared-resource ownership; focused testing; integration; and final verification. For each proposed lane, record its expected capacity, quality or coverage, elapsed-time, independence, and coordinator-integration value as defined below. Use two to five workers when that many meaningful lanes exist.\n\n### Tier 2: large or high-risk task\n\nUse the complete template in this file for repository-wide audits, large implementations, major refactors, multiple feature surfaces, database changes, migrations, deployment, high-risk security work, architecture changes, long-running or cross-computer work, tasks with six or more meaningful lanes, or work with substantial context/coordination-loss risk. Record the critical path and complete elapsed-time strategy, plus the expected capacity, quality or coverage, elapsed-time, independence, and coordinator-integration value of every proposed lane. The complete lane matrix and subagent communication plan are mandatory whenever workers are used. Target six to twelve direct workers when the dependency graph and installed capacity support that many real lanes.\n\n## Progressive planning rule\n\nMinimum safe preflight comes first. Identify immediately ready independent lanes, publish the required compact worker manifest, launch safe read-only lanes without unnecessary delay, and refine the dependency graph and detailed plan while they run. No write lane begins before its requirements, prerequisites, file/resource ownership, and integration boundaries are sufficiently established and every assigned path has passed the write-lane path-verification gate below. Launch write lanes as soon as those conditions are met, keep the highest-value ready work in available slots, and start newly unblocked work immediately. Plans are living coordination artifacts, not paperwork gates; make them detailed enough to protect quality without delaying safe useful work. \"Complete every planning table before starting any worker\" is prohibited when ready read-only lanes exist.\n\n## Planning principles\n\nAn execution plan must:\n\n- be self-contained enough for another coordinator to continue without rediscovering the task;\n- preserve the exact user goal, terminology, constraints, acceptance criteria, methodology, and output format;\n- separate requirements and invariants from implementation choices;\n- identify verified facts, assumptions, unknowns, and canonical sources;\n- represent work as a dependency graph rather than a falsely linear checklist;\n- identify the critical path and organize work to shorten it without weakening any hard constraint;\n- maximize safe, useful concurrency while identifying conflict-prone and serialized work;\n- establish one coordinator, clear lane ownership, and one writer per file at a time;\n- verify assigned write-lane paths against the current repository and distinguish verified existing files, intentional new files, and unresolved read-only paths;\n- identify shared runtime and expensive resources with authoritative owners;\n- define evidence, focused validation, integration validation, and completion criteria before implementation;\n- remain updateable as discoveries change scope, dependencies, ownership, risk, or validation;\n- make every subagent launch and material lifecycle change visible to the user through concise structured chat updates;\n- use completed results promptly and integrate safe independent work incrementally rather than waiting for unrelated lanes;\n- record significant decisions and discoveries without exposing private chain-of-thought; and\n- preserve completed work when the plan changes.\n\n## Capacity and wave scheduling\n\nRecord:\n\n```text\nConfigured open-thread capacity:\nCoordinator reservation:\nMaximum normal direct workers:\nCurrent ready lanes:\nCurrently active lanes:\nBlocked lanes:\nAvailable slots:\nNext ready lanes:\nHighest-value ready-lane queue:\nExclusive resources currently held:\n```\n\nKeep safe capacity filled with the highest-value ready meaningful work, reserve the coordinator when the client counts the root session, and maintain ready and next-ready queues for substantial tasks. Fill each newly available slot immediately rather than waiting for an entire wave. Capacity may remain intentionally unused only when no useful independent work exists or a concrete conflict, dependency, safety, or resource constraint prevents more work. When substantial ready work exists but a slot remains unused, record the reason briefly.\n\nDo not require a fixed worker count when fewer meaningful lanes exist, and do not create a lane merely because capacity is available. When enough real non-overlapping work exists, use the full safe and useful lane count. Avoid idle or artificial workers, stop or collapse redundant work after sufficient evidence exists, preserve results between waves, and keep completed high-priority output moving into review and integration.\n\n## Worker capability and reasoning-speed planning\n\nRoute work for both reliability and elapsed time. Use the fastest capable available worker configuration for mechanical inventories, repository searches, classifications, repetitive comparisons, and bounded read-only scans. Reserve high reasoning effort for work that actually needs it, and use stronger workers for difficult architecture, diagnosis, implementation, reconciliation, and verification. Do not assign maximum reasoning effort to every worker by habit.\n\nRecord any capability or reasoning requirement that materially affects lane success or urgency. ### Subagents started — Phase 3 warmup wave

All eight lanes are strictly read-only and provisional against the current dirty checkout. They may inspect the Phase 3 brief, governing docs, source, and tests, but may not edit, fetch/switch branches, install dependencies, start servers, mutate databases, run browser suites, or touch generated artifacts.

| Lane | Role | Status | Evidence ownership | End goal / deliverable | Non-scope | Dependencies / integration | Expected benefit |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| P3-R1 | Requirement intake auditor | Starting | Phase 3-assigned Codex/OA rows, audit matrix, roadmap, test plan | Zero-loss intake totals, ambiguities, and exact coverage-ledger inputs | No docs/source edits; no reclassification by guess | Rechecked after Phase 2 sync; coordinator owns final reconciliation | Moves the mandatory intake gate off the critical path |
| P3-R2 | Host and queue architect | Starting | Current director/provider/scene contracts plus Phase 3 host/queue brief | Dependency map and concrete persistent-host/priority/replay architecture | No implementation; no competing Phase 2 design | Must consume finalized Phase 2 interfaces before writes | Prepares the core architecture path early |
| P3-R3 | Event bridge/data-flow specialist | Starting | Quartermaster commands, APIs, SSE/snapshot/audit/domain events | End-to-end producer-to-Player event map with persistence and acknowledgment gaps | No DB/API mutations; no UI redesign | Coordinator reconciles with P3-R2 and Phase 2 handoff | Exposes backend/state blockers before UI work |
| P3-R4 | Journal specialist | Starting | Journal opening machine, PageFlip, pages, annotations, focus handoff | Verified opening/page lifecycle and integration plan | No non-Journal event families; no runtime | Depends on Phase 2 PageFlip/ownership contracts | Shortens the journal critical path |
| P3-R5 | Player surface specialist | Starting | Voyage Chart, altar/artifact, quests, log, finale components | Surface/event target inventory and missing-canonical-surface report | No Journal or global-host design; no edits | Coordinator splits later writers only after paths stabilize | Broadens event-family coverage concurrently |
| P3-R6 | Replay and resilience specialist | Starting | Replay/history, one-shot, reconnect, interruption, undo/revocation | Semantic policy and persistence test map | No UI styling or server/database mutation | Reconciled with event bridge and queue design | Finds correctness risks hidden by happy-path animation |
| P3-R7 | Accessibility and runtime-quality specialist | Starting | Focus, motion modes, audio independence, notifications, lifecycle/performance | Acceptance matrix and likely implementation/test gaps | No visual redesign, profiling runtime, or edits | Feeds every later implementation lane and final verifier | Adds independent cross-cutting verification |
| P3-R8 | Validation/isolation specialist | Starting | Existing Vitest/Playwright/config/isolation tooling and Phase 3 matrices | Executable focused/integrated test strategy, fixtures, ownership, and blockers | No tests run that mutate state; no server/build/full suite | Coordinator remains sole expensive-resource owner | Prevents late discovery of test and isolation gaps |

- **Capacity:** 8 workers launching; 5 direct-worker slots remain available.
- **Coordinator work:** complete brief reading, monitor the exact Phase 2 task, maintain the Tier 2 dependency/critical-path plan, protect concurrent diffs, review/reconcile findings, authorize any later write ownership, integrate, validate, synchronize, and report.
- **Authoritative shared-resource owner:** coordinator only for branches/Git integration, port `3000`, validation ports, browser automation, database/seed/migrations, dependencies/manifests/lockfiles, builds, E2E/full validation, generated assets, `Development_Docs`, and `Codex_Chats`.
- **Blocked/queued:** every Phase 3 write lane is blocked on Phase 2 completion and a fresh repository/diff/interface census. More lanes will start only if a distinct safe evidence boundary appears.
- I retain the original requirements, decomposition, dependencies, ownership/conflict prevention, major decisions, output review, contradiction resolution, final verification, and repository synchronization.

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 1.2 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 1 seconds\nOutput:\n# PROJECT LANTERNWAKE\r\n\r\n## Phase 3: Unfurl the Tale\r\n\r\n### Player Cinematic Integration, Persistent Progression Presentation, and Living Voyage Choreography\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 1. Mission\r\n\r\nImplement **Phase 3 of Project Lanternwake**, the complete animation-system rebuilding program for the Forever Treasure / Tall Tale experience.\r\n\r\nThis phase is called:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nUnfurl the Tale\r\n```\r\n\r\nIts formal engineering scope is:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPlayer Cinematic Integration, Persistent Progression Presentation, and Living Voyage Choreography\r\n```\r\n\r\nPhase 1, **Light the Lantern**, established whether an animation presentation is truthful:\r\n\r\n* typed target contracts;\r\n* visible and unique target validation;\r\n* structured presentation outcomes;\r\n* acknowledgment safety;\r\n* persisted presentation replay;\r\n* bounded journal lifecycle behavior;\r\n* one authoritative reduced-motion policy;\r\n* and explicit scene reachability.\r\n\r\nPhase 2, **Claim the Deck**, established where animation may occur and which runtime owns it:\r\n\r\n* dedicated SceneHosts;\r\n* scene-instance identity;\r\n* host-local target resolution;\r\n* explicit external target handles;\r\n* enforced runtime ownership;\r\n* Motion/GSAP wrapper separation;\r\n* PageFlip source and clone boundaries;\r\n* final-state handoff;\r\n* and collision-safe component architecture.\r\n\r\nPhase 3 must now use those foundations to create one coherent, persistent and fully testable Player cinematic experience.\r\n\r\nThe central rule of this phase is:\r\n\r\n> Every authoritative Player event must produce one visible, readable, replayable and correctly acknowledged presentation regardless of which Player section is currently open.\r\n\r\nThe current Player section must never determine whether an authoritative event can be seen.\r\n\r\nA live event must never be considered presented merely because:\r\n\r\n* a timeline resolved;\r\n* a temporary overlay mounted;\r\n* a sound played;\r\n* a snapshot refreshed;\r\n* a hidden PageFlip source matched;\r\n* an unrelated section happened to contain the same part Required Phase 3 Design Record\r\n\r\nBefore broad implementation, create:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_3_Design_Record.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe design record must define and freeze:\r\n\r\n1. `ProgressionSceneHost`\r\n2. `ProgressionPresentationController`\r\n3. `ProgressionPresentationQueue`\r\n4. `ProgressionPresentationRequest`\r\n5. `ProgressionPresentationPriority`\r\n6. `ProgressionPresentationStatus`\r\n7. `ProgressionPresentationReceipt`\r\n8. `PlayerSectionId`\r\n9. `PlayerSectionRestoration`\r\n10. `GlobalProgressionPresentation`\r\n11. `SectionLocalEnhancement`\r\n12. `ReplayPresentationRequest`\r\n13. `ProgressionEventPresentationPolicy`\r\n14. `ProgressionAudioLabelMap`\r\n15. `ProgressionFallbackPolicy`\r\n16. `OpeningPresentationPolicy`\r\n17. `JournalReadyReceipt`\r\n18. `PageTurnLifecycleEvent`\r\n19. `ProgressionNotificationPolicy`\r\n20. `OfflineReconciliationPresentation`\r\n\r\nThe design record must explain:\r\n\r\n* why the global host remains mounted outside conditional sections;\r\n* how global presentation differs from local enhancement;\r\n* which layer owns acknowledgment;\r\n* how event sequence determines queue order;\r\n* how duplicate event IDs are ignored;\r\n* how replay differs from live presentation;\r\n* how current section is preserved;\r\n* how focus is restored;\r\n* how a section-local settled state receives explicit handoff;\r\n* how audio waits for visual preflight;\r\n* how reduced mode preserves semantic order;\r\n* how Rive fallbacks participate before Phase 5;\r\n* how interruption and reconnect work;\r\n* how all Phase 3 OA and MX requirements are mapped.\r\n\r\nDo not begin parallel event-family implementation until the global host, event policy and queue interfaces are stable.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Workstream A: Persistent Player ProgressionSceneHost\r\n\r\nCreate a persistent Player cinematic host outside conditional Player section content.\r\n\r\nThe host must remain mounted while the Player switches among:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nJournal\r\nVoyage Chart\r\nTreasure Altar\r\nSide-Quest Ledger\r\nShipâ€™s Log\r\nFinale Chamber\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Host responsibilities\r\n\r\nThe persistent host owns:\r\n\r\n* global authoritative event presentation;\r\n* scene-instance creation;\r\n* event queue;\r\n* replay presentation;\r\n* global fallback;\r\n* presentation receipt;\r\n* acknowledgment eligibility;\r\n* focus capture and restoration;\r\n* current-section preservation;\r\n* sound-label dispatch;\r\n* interruption and skip;\r\n* overlay stacking;\r\n* cleanup.\r\n\r\n## Host must not own\r\n\r\nThe global host must not become:\r\n\r\n* the authoritative business-state store;\r\n* a duplicate event store;\r\n* a second snapshot source;\r\n* a replacement for section content;\r\n* a global DOM dumping ground;\r\n* a forced-navigation mechanism.\r\n\r\n## Required structure\r\n\r\nThe exact component design may vary, but it must provide equivalent capability:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ntype ProgressionSceneHostProps = {\r\n  campaignId: string;\r\n  currentSection: PlayerSectionId;\r\n  onRequestSection(section: PlayerSectionId): void;\r\n  onPresentationReceipt(receipt: PresentationReceipt): void;\r\n  onAcknowledgmentEligible(eventId: string): void;\r\n};\r\n\r\ntype ProgressionPresentationRequest = {\r\n  eventId: string;\r\n  eventSequence: number;\r\n  eventType: ProgressEventType;\r\n  sceneName: SceneName;\r\n  payload: PlayerSafePresentationPayload;\r\n  source: \"live\" | \"replay\" | \"reconnect\";\r\n  requestedMotionPolicy: ResolvedMotionPolicy;\r\n  returnSection: PlayerSectionId;\r\n};\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Required host layers\r\n\r\nUse explicit internal layers:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nbackdrop/dimming layer\r\nprimary ceremony layer\r\nevent-object layer\r\ntext/readable-summary layer\r\ncontrols layer\r\nlive-region layer\r\nfallback layer\r\n```\r\n\r\nEach layer must have declared runtime ownership.\r\n\r\n## Stacking and interaction\r\n\r\nThe host must:\r\n\r\n* remain above section content when active;\r\n* not permanently block section controls after completion;\r\n* not trap focus unless the active presentation is modal;\r\n* expose Skip only when policy permits;\r\n* expose Replay only for replayable presentations;\r\n* restore focus after completion or cancellation;\r\n* preserve section scroll position when appropriate.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 11. Workstream B: Progression Event Queue and Priority\r\n\r\nImplement one deterministic queue for live Player progression.\r\n\r\n## Required queue behavior\r\n\r\nThe queue must:\r\n\r\n* order events by authoritative event sequence;\r\n* deduplicate by immutable event ID;\r\n* distinguish live, replay and reconnect requests;\r\n* prevent overlapping mandatory scenes;\r\n* permit low-cost section-local enhancements only when ownership allows;\r\n* pause or cancel ambient work before mandatory scenes;\r\n* support skip policy;\r\n* support interruption policy;\r\n* survive section changes;\r\n* survive host rerenders;\r\n* reconcile after reconnect;\r\n* avoid infinite retry loops.\r\n\r\n## Recommended priority classes\r\n\r\nAt minimum:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncritical-authoritative\r\nmandatory-ceremony\r\nstate-transition\r\ninformational\r\ndecorative\r\nmanual-replay\r\n```\r\n\r\nSuggested examples:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCHAPTER_RELEASED -> mandatory-ceremony\r\nFINALE unlock -> critical-authoritative\r\nCAMPAIGN_PAUSED -> state-transition\r\nPLAYER_LOG_ENTRY_ADDED -> informational\r\nambient pulse -> decorative\r\nmanual chapter replay -> manual-replay\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Live event versus replay\r\n\r\nLive authoritative events take priority over manual replay unless the current replay is at a non-interruptible semantic boundary.\r\n\r\nIf interrupted:\r\n\r\n* replay never acknowledges anything;\r\n* live event remains authoritative;\r\n* the interrupted replay normalizes before cleanup;\r\n* the user may replay it again later.\r\n\r\n## Duplicate events\r\n\r\nA duplicate event ID must:\r\n\r\n* not create another scene instance;\r\n* not create another acknowledgment;\r\n* not replay audio;\r\n* not alter queue order;\r\n* be recorded as a duplicate receipt.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 12. Workstream C: Player Event Presentation Policy Table\r\n\r\nCreate a typed, exhaustive mapping for every Player progression event.\r\n\r\nThe 17 event types are:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCHAPTER_RELEASED\r\nCHAPTER_SOLVED\r\nARTIFACT_AWARDED\r\nARTIFACT_SILHOUETTE_REVEALED\r\nARTIFACT_CONNECTED\r\nMAP_LOCATION_REVEALED\r\nMAP_ROUTE_REVEALED\r\nSIDE_QUEST_DISCOVERED\r\nSIDE_QUEST_UPDATED\r\nSIDE_QUEST_COMPLETED\r\nJOURNAL_ANNOTATION_ADDED\r\nPLAYER_LOG_ENTRY_ADDED\r\nFINALE_TEASED\r\nFINALE_REQUIREMENT_UPDATED\r\nCAMPAIGN_PAUSED\r\nCAMPAIGN_RESUMED\r\nSTATE_REVERTED\r\n```\r\n\r\nFor every event define:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nscene name\r\npriority\r\nmandatory/optional status\r\nglobal presentation\r\nrelevant section\r\nlocal enhancement\r\nrequired targets\r\nfallback\r\nreplay policy\r\none-shot scope\r\nskip policy\r\nacknowledgment policy\r\naudio labels\r\nduration budget\r\nreduced behavior\r\nfocus behavior\r\nnotification behavior\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe mapping must be exhaustive.\r\n\r\nA new event type must fail typecheck or tests until its presentation policy is declared.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 13. Workstream D: Rebuild Chapter Release\r\n\r\n`CHAPTER_RELEASED` is the central Phase 3 ceremony and a P0 release gate.\r\n\r\nRebuild it on the persistent global host.\r\n\r\nIt must no longer depend on `JournalWorkspace` being mounted.\r\n\r\n## Required full-mode semantic stages\r\n\r\nAt minimum:\r\n\r\n```text\r\npreflight\r\nattention\r\nworkspace-dim\r\nseal-pressure\r\nseal-fracture\r\nparchment-unfold\r\nchapter-heading\r\nprose-introduction\r\nprose-story\r\nobjective-placement\r\nriddle-reveal\r\nquill-writing\r\nmap-inset\r\ncompletion\r\nactions-ready\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe exact visual timing may be tuned, but the semantic order must remain explicit and testable.\r\n\r\n## Required visible components\r\n\r\nThe global ceremony must contain dedicated host-local targets for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nworkspace dim\r\nsealed parchment\r\nseal\r\nseal cracks\r\nwax fragments\r\nchapter heading\r\nstory prose\r\nobjective card\r\nriddle region\r\nquill\r\nink path\r\nmap inset\r\ncompletion state\r\nOpen Chapter action\r\nReturn action\r\nReplay state\r\nSkip state\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not use Journal section content as required ceremony targets.\r\n\r\n## Off-Journal behavior\r\n\r\nWhen release arrives while the Player is in:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nVoyage Chart\r\nTreasure Altar\r\nSide-Quest Ledger\r\nShipâ€™s Log\r\nFinale Chamber\r\n```\r\n\r\nthe ceremony must:\r\n\r\n1. play globally;\r\n2. preserve the current section;\r\n3. not force navigation;\r\n4. not unmount the current section;\r\n5. offer an explicit Open Chapter or Return to Journal action afterward;\r\n6. restore the prior section if the user dismisses;\r\n7. preserve replay availability.\r\n\r\n## Journal behavior\r\n\r\nWhen release arrives while already in Journal:\r\n\r\n* the global ceremony still owns authoritative presentation;\r\n* the Journal may receive a local settled-state enhancement;\r\n* the Journal cannot become a second cinematic owner;\r\n* acknowledgment belongs to the global hostâ€™s receipt.\r\n\r\n## Timing budget\r\n\r\nFull mode must complete from validated trigger to interactive final state in:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nstrictly less than 10,000 ms\r\n```\r\n\r\nTarget design:\r\n\r\n```text\r\n5,000 to 9,000 ms\r\n```\r\n\r\nReduced mode must complete significantly faster while preserving semantic order.\r\n\r\n## Required fallback\r\n\r\nIf GSAP, Lottie or a visual asset fails:\r\n\r\n* render a readable chapter-release card;\r\n* include chapter title, Player-visible text, objective and riddle;\r\n* expose Open Chapter;\r\n* expose Replay when allowed;\r\n* return `presented-fallback`;\r\n* acknowledge only if policy permits.\r\n\r\nA missing optional fog, quill flourish or fragment may not block the readable ceremony.\r\n\r\nA missing required readable region must block acknowledgment.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 14. Workstream E: Chapter Release Additional Animations\r\n\r\nImplement every accepted Phase 3 chapter-release requirement, including distinct behavior for:\r\n\r\n* global ceremony host;\r\n* current-section awareness;\r\n* seal-pressure anticipation;\r\n* synchronized wax fracture;\r\n* parchment unfolding;\r\n* heading handwriting;\r\n* prose ink progression;\r\n* objective card placement;\r\n* riddle reveal;\r\n* quill-followed writing;\r\n* map inset reveal;\r\n* Open Chapter action transition;\r\n* previous-section restoration;\r\n* replay from persisted history;\r\n* gentle-mode shortened composition;\r\n* reduced-mode semantic staged reveal.\r\n\r\nDo not collapse these into one matrix row without implementing and testing each semantic requirement.\r\n\r\n## Gentle mode\r\n\r\nGentle mode must not merely multiply all durations by a constant.\r\n\r\nIt should:\r\n\r\n* reduce travel distance;\r\n* shorten pauses;\r\n* reduce fragment count;\r\n* minimize rotation;\r\n* preserve seal, parchment, heading, prose and objective order;\r\n* keep readable pacing.\r\n\r\n## Reduced mode\r\n\r\nReduced mode must:\r\n\r\n* avoid large movement;\r\n* avoid parallax;\r\n* avoid long seal-fragment travel;\r\n* avoid quill motion paths;\r\n* preserve ordered state changes;\r\n* reveal readable content in semantic groups;\r\n* retain completion and actions;\r\n* preserve sound preference independently.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 15. Workstream I: Side-Quest Events\r\n\r\nImplement complete presentation for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nSIDE_QUEST_DISCOVERED\r\nSIDE_QUEST_UPDATED\r\nSIDE_QUEST_COMPLETED\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Discovery\r\n\r\nGlobal presentation:\r\n\r\n* folded rumor note;\r\n* readable quest title;\r\n* destination action;\r\n* optional pin/thread visual.\r\n\r\nLedger-local enhancement:\r\n\r\n* insert or reveal exactly one quest;\r\n* preserve current page;\r\n* use explicit quest ID;\r\n* draw only relevant thread;\r\n* no full PageFlip destruction.\r\n\r\n## Update\r\n\r\nGlobal presentation:\r\n\r\n* readable changed-objective summary.\r\n\r\nLedger-local enhancement:\r\n\r\n* underline or ink only the changed objective;\r\n* update partial-completion state;\r\n* preserve card and page identity;\r\n* do not reuse discovery choreography blindly.\r\n\r\n## Completion\r\n\r\nGlobal presentation:\r\n\r\n* explicit completion receipt;\r\n* reward summary if Player-visible.\r\n\r\nLedger-local enhancement:\r\n\r\n* completion stamp on exact quest;\r\n* thread relaxation;\r\n* reward pocket reveal where assigned;\r\n* completed-note repositioning;\r\n* stable final state.\r\n\r\nImplement all accepted Phase 3 Side-Quest requirements including applicable:\r\n\r\n* ledger opening;\r\n* physical divider movement;\r\n* rumor-note arrival;\r\n* pin insertion;\r\n* red-thread drawing;\r\n* checkbox ink;\r\n* partial completion;\r\n* completion stamp;\r\n* reward pocket;\r\n* completed-note repositioning;\r\n* page preservation;\r\n* no unnecessary PageFlip recreation.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 19. semantic state interface intended for Rive;\r\n* do not claim the fallback is live Rive;\r\n* keep scene orchestration production-ready;\r\n* test both fallback and future runtime interface.\r\n\r\n## Workstream L: Campaign Pause, Resume, and Undo\r\n\r\nImplement complete global presentation for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCAMPAIGN_PAUSED\r\nCAMPAIGN_RESUMED\r\nSTATE_REVERTED\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Pause\r\n\r\nRequired:\r\n\r\n* global status transition;\r\n* readable paused state;\r\n* Player controls reconcile;\r\n* ambient motion reduces or stops according to policy;\r\n* no generic root-wide lantern query;\r\n* section remains mounted;\r\n* pause state persists.\r\n\r\n## Resume\r\n\r\nRequired:\r\n\r\n* authoritative resumed state;\r\n* ambient policy restores deliberately;\r\n* no replay of prior ceremonies;\r\n* controls reconcile;\r\n* one transition per state version.\r\n\r\n## Undo/state reverted\r\n\r\nRequired:\r\n\r\n* readable previous-versus-current summary;\r\n* explicit affected object identities;\r\n* local section state reconciliation;\r\n* no fake reverse of unrelated scene;\r\n* event history remains truthful;\r\n* replay is presentation-only.\r\n\r\nIf Quartermaster command preview is assigned to Phase 3, it must display the exact previous event/state and cannot rely on a generic undo mark.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 22. Workstream O: Journal Page Experience\r\n\r\nComplete all accepted Phase 3 page-level behavior while preserving StPageFlip ownership.\r\n\r\n## StPageFlip owns\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncurl\r\ndrag\r\nmanual turn\r\nkeyboard turn\r\nprogrammatic turn\r\npage index\r\norientation\r\nturn lifecycle\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Motion owns\r\n\r\n```text\r\nchapter tab layout\r\nobjective note presence\r\nhint presence\r\nbookmark movement\r\npage-local dialogs\r\n```\r\n\r\n## GSAP owns\r\n\r\n```text\r\nbounded annotation ink\r\nillustration reveal\r\nchapter divider flourish\r\ncompletion stamp child\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Required PageFlip lifecycle\r\n\r\nEmit:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nturn-start\r\nturn-commit\r\nturn-settle\r\nturn-cancel\r\nturn-failed\r\n```\r\n\r\nAudio and page-local effects must use lifecycle labels rather than button-request timing.\r\n\r\n## Implement applicable accepted requirements\r\n\r\n* page-edge hover lift;\r\n* drag-intent affordance;\r\n* midpoint shadow;\r\n* chapter-tab-to-page handoff;\r\n* queued-turn indicator;\r\n* chapter divider ribbon;\r\n* annotation handwriting;\r\n* illustration reveal;\r\n* bookmark movement;\r\n* current-objective highlight;\r\n* hint foldout;\r\n* read/unread ink;\r\n* completed chapter stamp;\r\n* historical watermark;\r\n* safe locked-page treatment.\r\n\r\n## Reduced mode\r\n\r\nReduced mode may replace physical curl with an accessible static page change.\r\n\r\nIt must preserve:\r\n\r\n* page index;\r\n* chapter tab;\r\n* focus;\r\n* announcement;\r\n* objective;\r\n* replay;\r\n* controls.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 25. Workstream P: Section-Local Enhancements\r\n\r\nEvery authoritative event gets:\r\n\r\n```text\r\none global presentation\r\nplus at most one relevant section-local enhancement\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe local enhancement is optional.\r\n\r\nIt cannot own:\r\n\r\n* event acknowledgment;\r\n* global replay receipt;\r\n* event queue;\r\n* authoritative fallback.\r\n\r\n## Section mapping\r\n\r\n### Journal\r\n\r\nLocal enhancements for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCHAPTER_RELEASED\r\nCHAPTER_SOLVED\r\nJOURNAL_ANNOTATION_ADDED\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Voyage Chart\r\n\r\nLocal enhancements for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nMAP_LOCATION_REVEALED\r\nMAP_ROUTE_REVEALED\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Treasure Altar\r\n\r\nLocal enhancements for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nARTIFACT_AWARDED\r\nARTIFACT_SILHOUETTE_REVEALED\r\nARTIFACT_CONNECTED\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Side-Quest Ledger\r\n\r\nLocal enhancements for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nSIDE_QUEST_DISCOVERED\r\nSIDE_QUEST_UPDATED\r\nSIDE_QUEST_COMPLETED\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Shipâ€™s Log\r\n\r\nLocal enhancements for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPLAYER_LOG_ENTRY_ADDED\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Finale Chamber\r\n\r\nLocal enhancements for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nFINALE_TEASED\r\nFINALE_REQUIREMENT_UPDATED\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Global-only unless explicitly needed\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCAMPAIGN_PAUSED\r\nCAMPAIGN_RESUMED\r\nSTATE_REVERTED\r\n```\r\n\r\n## No duplicate cinematic ownership\r\n\r\nIf a section-local enhancement is running:\r\n\r\n* it uses its own host;\r\n* it receives an explicit handoff;\r\n* it cannot play the same global scene again;\r\n* it cannot acknowledge the event;\r\n* it cannot steal global targets;\r\n* it must remain readable if skipped.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 26. Workstream Q: Replay and Historical Presentation\r\n\r\nUse Phase 1 persisted Player-safe event history.\r\n\r\nReplay must work:\r\n\r\n1. immediately after live presentation;\r\n2. repeatedly;\r\n3. from another Player section;\r\n4. after navigation;\r\n5. after browser refresh;\r\n6. after route remount;\r\n7. after motion-mode change;\r\n8. after skip;\r\n9. after interruption;\r\n10. after reconnect.\r\n\r\n## Replay rules\r\n\r\nReplay:\r\n\r\n* creates a new presentation instance;\r\n* uses the same immutable event payload;\r\n* does not create a new event;\r\n* does not create a command;\r\n* does not advance progression;\r\n* does not create another viewed receipt;\r\n* uses current motion policy;\r\n* restores prior section;\r\n* restores focus;\r\n* cleans up fully.\r\n\r\n## Replay history interface\r\n\r\nProvide a safe Player-facing replay surface for every event whose policy allows replay.\r\n\r\nAt minimum, replayable historical presentations should include:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nchapter release\r\nchapter solved where useful\r\nartifact award\r\nartifact connection\r\nmap location\r\nmap route\r\nquest discovery/completion\r\nnew log entry\r\nfinale tease/requirement\r\nfinale completion\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not expose private Captain-only payload fields.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 27. Workstream R: Audio Synchronization\r\n\r\nMove Player progression audio to named semantic labels after visible-target validation.\r\n\r\nAudio must never fire merely because an event handler started.\r\n\r\n## Required label examples\r\n\r\n```text\r\nseal-pressure\r\nseal-fracture\r\nparchment-open\r\nink-heading\r\nink-story\r\nobjective-settle\r\nriddle-reveal\r\nmarker-commit\r\nroute-draw-start\r\nroute-draw-complete\r\nartifact-reveal-settle\r\nartifact-connection-complete\r\nquest-note-arrive\r\nquest-stamp-impact\r\nlog-ink-start\r\nfinale-wake\r\nrequirement-commit\r\npause-commit\r\nresume-commit\r\nreconciliation-commit\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Audio rules\r\n\r\n* visual preflight first;\r\n* cue at semantic label;\r\n* mute respected;\r\n* volume respected;\r\n* sound preference independent from motion preference;\r\n* Web Audio failure non-blocking;\r\n* no audio-only meaning;\r\n* no duplicate cue on duplicate event;\r\n* replay follows replay policy;\r\n* mode changes do not replay one-shots.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 28. Workstream S: Lottie Integration\r\n\r\nLottie remains a contained illustrative runtime.\r\n\r\nFor Phase 3:\r\n\r\n* ambient fog may support global ceremony atmosphere;\r\n* ink bloom may support chapter/prose stages;\r\n* Lottie never controls external state;\r\n* one-shot Lottie begins only at a director label;\r\n* mode change does not recreate or autoplay;\r\n* hidden document pauses loops;\r\n* reduced mode uses a deliberate representative frame;\r\n* stalled load has a bounded fallback.\r\n\r\nThe chapter ink effect must not autoplay on mount.\r\n\r\nIt must begin at its named ink stage.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 29. Workstream T: Rive Fallback Integration\r\n\r\nPhase 5 authors the production Rive binaries.\r\n\r\nPhase 3 must integrate their semantic interfaces now.\r\n\r\nExpected future objects:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nJournal Clasp\r\nVoyage Compass\r\nFinale Mechanism\r\n```\r\n\r\nInvitation Seal belongs primarily to Phase 4.\r\n\r\n## Phase 3 requirements\r\n\r\nFor each Player object:\r\n\r\n* define stable state names;\r\n* define input contract;\r\n* define reduced pose;\r\n* define fallback;\r\n* mount the correct fallback where the object is part of Phase 3;\r\n* emit runtime/fallback truth;\r\n* do not block the scene if the optional authored asset is absent;\r\n* do not claim live Rive success.\r\n\r\nThe final Phase 5 binary should be installable without redesigning Player scene state.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 30. Workstream U: Offline, Reconnect, and Interrupted Events\r\n\r\nImplement a safe Player presentation policy for:\r\n\r\n* connection lost before an event;\r\n* connection lost during a scene;\r\n* connection lost before acknowledgment;\r\n* multiple missed events during disconnection;\r\n* reconnect with snapshot/event catch-up;\r\n* duplicate replayed SSE event;\r\n* stale event;\r\n* revoked access.\r\n\r\n## Reconnect behavior\r\n\r\nAfter reconnect:\r\n\r\n1. fetch authoritative state;\r\n2. identify unseen Player-safe events;\r\n3. order them by sequence;\r\n4. present a readable reconciliation summary;\r\n5. queue mandatory events;\r\n6. avoid duplicate presentations;\r\n7. preserve replay;\r\n8. acknowledge only after valid presentation.\r\n\r\n## Interrupted scene\r\n\r\nOffer policy-driven behavior:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nresume\r\nrestart presentation\r\nskip to readable final state\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not blindly continue a stale timeline against reconciled DOM.\r\n\r\n## Revocation\r\n\r\nAccess revocation must:\r\n\r\n* terminate reconnect retry;\r\n* stop presentation queue safely;\r\n* render a terminal readable state;\r\n* preserve no private cached content beyond approved policy;\r\n* not masquerade as ordinary offline behavior.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 31. Workstream V: Focus, Navigation, and Accessibility\r\n\r\nThe global host must have explicit focus behavior.\r\n\r\n## Before presentation\r\n\r\nCapture:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncurrent section\r\nactive element\r\nsection scroll state\r\nrelevant trigger\r\n```\r\n\r\n## During modal ceremony\r\n\r\n* move focus to the ceremony heading or container;\r\n* expose Skip when allowed;\r\n* keep controls keyboard reachable;\r\n* trap focus only when truly modal;\r\n* announce event type and current status;\r\n* hide decorative layers from assistive technology.\r\n\r\n## After presentation\r\n\r\nRestore focus to:\r\n\r\n* the prior meaningful element;\r\n* or the explicit destination action;\r\n* or the active section heading.\r\n\r\nDo not restore focus to:\r\n\r\n* a detached node;\r\n* a hidden PageFlip source;\r\n* an unmounted section;\r\n* the document body without explanation.\r\n\r\n## Reduced motion\r\n\r\nReduced motion must preserve:\r\n\r\n* event order;\r\n* text;\r\n* actions;\r\n* focus;\r\n* live-region announcements;\r\n* replay;\r\n* section restoration.\r\n\r\n## Sound independence\r\n\r\nEvery event type must remain fully understandable with sound disabled.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 32. Workstream W: Notifications and Unseen Content\r\n\r\nThe global cinematic host and ordinary notification system must remain distinct.\r\n\r\n## Cinematic presentation\r\n\r\nUse for:\r\n\r\n* mandatory or narratively significant events;\r\n* replayable ceremonies;\r\n* server-authoritative progress transitions.\r\n\r\n## Ordinary notification\r\n\r\nUse for:\r\n\r\n* low-priority informational changes;\r\n* destination reminders;\r\n* viewed status;\r\n* queue summaries.\r\n\r\nImplement:\r\n\r\n* unseen section badges;\r\n* unseen count decrement;\r\n* global event notification;\r\n* serialized notification queue;\r\n* viewed acknowledgment feedback.\r\n\r\nDo not pulse unseen badges indefinitely.\r\n\r\nDo not let notification animation acknowledge the underlying event.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 33. Step 13: Final validation\r\n\r\nOne owner runs integrated gates.\r\n\r\n## Step 14: Synchronization and stop\r\n\r\nSynchronize:\r\n\r\n* Development_Docs;\r\n* Codex chat archive;\r\n* validation artifacts.\r\n\r\nStop for review.\r\n\r\nDo not begin Phase 4 automatically.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 46. Parallelization Rules\r\n\r\nParallelize aggressively after the shared Player host and event contract are frozen.\r\n\r\n## Single-owner core files\r\n\r\nOnly one owner may edit:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPlayerExperience.tsx\r\nProgressionSceneHost implementation\r\nevent queue\r\nacknowledgment integration\r\nreplay integration\r\nstory.scene.ts shared orchestration\r\ncommand.scene.ts shared orchestration\r\naudio cue registry\r\nPlayer global CSS\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Safe parallel lanes\r\n\r\n### Lane A\r\n\r\n```text\r\nVoyageChart.tsx\r\nmap/route scene modules\r\nmap-focused tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Lane B\r\n\r\n```text\r\nTreasureAltar.tsx\r\nArtifactInspection.tsx\r\nartifact scene modules\r\nartifact tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Lane C\r\n\r\n```text\r\nSideQuestLedger.tsx\r\nquest scene modules\r\nquest tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Lane D\r\n\r\n```text\r\nJournalWorkspace.tsx\r\nShipsLog.tsx\r\nannotation/log modules\r\nfocused tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Lane E\r\n\r\n```text\r\nFinaleChamber.tsx\r\nfinale scene modules\r\nfinale tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Lane F\r\n\r\n```text\r\npause/resume/undo presentation modules\r\ncampaign-state tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Lane G\r\n\r\n```text\r\nQuartermaster event bridge\r\ncommand receipt tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Lane H\r\n\r\nRead-only accessibility and requirement-traceability review.\r\n\r\n## Serialized resources\r\n\r\nOnly one owner controls:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nport 3000\r\nvalidation ports\r\nbrowser automation\r\ndatabase\r\nseed\r\nmigrations\r\nSSE mutation fixture\r\nfull E2E\r\nproduction build\r\nnpm run validate\r\n```\r\n\r\nNo parallel lane may create a competing global Player host.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 47. Runtime and Database Isolation\r\n\r\nDo not run mutating Player/Quartermaster E2E until database isolation is proven.\r\n\r\nRequired:\r\n\r\n1. create uniquely named database copy;\r\n2. use explicit absolute `DATABASE_URL`;\r\n3. record server PID and port;\r\n4. store isolation nonce in copied DB;\r\n5. query nonce through application;\r\n6. record canonical DB path/hash/size/modification time;\r\n7. run mutations only on the copy;\r\n8. verify canonical DB remains unchanged.\r\n\r\nAn alternate worktree or port is not database isolation.\r\n\r\nIf proof fails:\r\n\r\n* stop mutation tests;\r\n* classify as environment failure;\r\n* do not continue by assumption.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 48. Required Validation Report\r\n\r\nCreate:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_3_Validation_Report.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nRecord exact commands, exit codes and results for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nformat\r\nlint\r\ntypecheck\r\nunit tests\r\ncomponent tests\r\ntarget-integrity tests\r\nopening tests\r\nPageFlip tests\r\nchapter-release tests\r\nevent-family tests\r\n102-case matrix\r\nreplay tests\r\nmode tests\r\nviewport tests\r\ninterruption tests\r\noffline/reconnect tests\r\naccessibility tests\r\nperformance tests\r\nlifecycle tests\r\nbuild\r\nE2E\r\nfull validation\r\n```\r\n\r\nClassify failures:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ntask-regression\r\npre-existing\r\nenvironment\r\ndatabase-isolation\r\nmissing-phase-5-asset\r\nblocked\r\nunresolved\r\n```\r\n\r\nA skipped or blocked test is not a pass.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 52. Validation Commands\r\n\r\nUse verified repository commands only.\r\n\r\nAt minimum run appropriate focused and full versions of:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nnpm run format:check\r\nnpm run lint\r\nnpm run typecheck\r\nnpm test\r\nnpm run assets:validate\r\nnpm run test:e2e\r\nnpm run build\r\nnpm run validate\r\n```\r\n\r\nOnly one validation owner runs the integrated full gate.\r\n\r\nDo not run multiple production builds or full E2E suites concurrently.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 53. Phase 3 Acceptance Criteria\r\n\r\nUnfurl the Tale is complete only when every condition below is satisfied.\r\n\r\n## Persistent host\r\n\r\n* One persistent Player progression host exists outside conditional section content.\r\n* It remains mounted across all six sections.\r\n* It owns authoritative Player presentation.\r\n* It does not become a second business-state store.\r\n* It restores section and focus.\r\n\r\n## Chapter release\r\n\r\n* Visible from all six sections.\r\n* Does not force navigation.\r\n* Required targets are host-local and unique.\r\n* Replay survives refresh.\r\n* Replay causes zero mutation.\r\n* Full mode completes in under 10,000 ms.\r\n* Gentle and reduced preserve meaning.\r\n* Fallback is readable.\r\n* Acknowledgment follows valid receipt only.\r\n\r\n## Event coverage\r\n\r\n* All 17 progression events have explicit typed policies.\r\n* All 102 full-mode event/section cases pass.\r\n* Every event has a global presentation.\r\n* Relevant sections receive at most one local enhancement.\r\n* Off-section absence cannot make an event invisible.\r\n* Duplicate event IDs do not replay.\r\n\r\n## Journal\r\n\r\n* First opening, reentry, replay, skip, interruption, completed archive and fallback end at `JOURNAL_READY`.\r\n* PageFlip handoff is stable.\r\n* Hidden source content never satisfies a scene.\r\n* Page turns expose lifecycle labels.\r\n* Focus and page index remain correct.\r\n\r\n## Section choreography\r\n\r\n* Map events animate only changed marker/path.\r\n* Artifact events use explicit source/destination handles.\r\n* Quest events animate only the affected quest/objective.\r\n* Log events use Motion row wrappers and GSAP ink children.\r\n* Finale events animate only changed requirement/mechanism state.\r\n* Pause/resume/undo reconcile globally and safely.\r\n\r\n## Replay\r\n\r\n* Every replayable Phase 3 presentation passes the full replay protocol.\r\n* Replay works after refresh.\r\n* Replay works from any section.\r\n* Replay uses immutable safe payloads.\r\n* Replay creates no story mutation or additional viewed receipt.\r\n\r\n## Audio\r\n\r\n* Audio begins only at semantic labels after preflight.\r\n* Sound failure is non-blocking.\r\n* Sound is never the only signal.\r\n* Duplicate events do not duplicate cues.\r\n\r\n## Motion policy\r\n\r\n* Full, gentle, product reduced, browser reduced and combined reduced reach equivalent semantic states.\r\n* Mode changes do not accidentally replay scenes or one-shot Lottie.\r\n* Reduced mode remains readable and interactive.\r\n\r\n## Accessibility\r\n\r\n* Keyboard access passes.\r\n* Focus restoration passes.\r\n* Live-region behavior passes.\r\n* No hidden PageFlip source is focusable.\r\n* No hover-only information exists.\r\n* Sound-independent meaning passes.\r\n\r\n## Lifecycle\r\n\r\n* Hosts, claims, runtimes, listeners and timers return to baseline.\r\n* No stale scene instance remains.\r\n* No leaked focus trap remains.\r\n* No detached-node writes occur.\r\n* Twenty-cycle stress tests pass.\r\n\r\n## Requirement completion\r\n\r\n* Every accepted Phase 3 MX requirement is implemented or has an explicit external blocker.\r\n* Every accepted Phase 3 OA requirement is implemented or has an explicit external blocker.\r\n* No architecture-only placeholder is falsely marked complete.\r\n* Zero accepted Phase 3 requirements remain unmapped.\r\n* Zero accepted Phase 3 requirements silently move to another phase.\r\n* All later-phase requirements remain preserved.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 54. Explicitly Out of Scope\r\n\r\nDo not broadly implement Phase 4 platform work during this phase:\r\n\r\n* modern sign-in polish;\r\n* Invitation Ceremony;\r\n* Player Library;\r\n* Captain Library;\r\n* New Voyage wizard;\r\n* waiting-room platform choreography unless required for Player journal handoff;\r\n* Studio animation;\r\n* shell-wide platform transitions.\r\n\r\nDo not broadly implement Phase 5 authored assets:\r\n\r\n* production Journal Clasp `.riv`;\r\n* production Voyage Compass `.riv`;\r\n* production Finale Mechanism `.riv`;\r\n* production Invitation Seal `.riv`;\r\n* final Lottie asset revisions;\r\n* final PageFlip runtime completion beyond required Phase 3 integration.\r\n\r\nDo not broadly implement Phase 6 final optimization and polish.\r\n\r\nDo not:\r\n\r\n* replace GSAP with Motion;\r\n* replace Motion with GSAP;\r\n* replace StPageFlip;\r\n* fake Rive with unlabeled CSS while claiming Rive completion;\r\n* expose private event content;\r\n* change story logic;\r\n* change authoritative progression semantics;\r\n* create a competing application;\r\n* start Phase 4 automatically.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 55. Phase 4 Handoff\r\n\r\nAt completion, provide Phase 4, **Bring the Harbor Alive**, with stable reusable interfaces for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nplatform ceremony host\r\nMotion route-presence policy\r\naccess/login final-state handoff\r\nnotification queue integration\r\nfocus restoration\r\nresolved motion policy\r\nfallback presentation\r\nsemantic audio labels\r\nRive fallback truth\r\n```\r\n\r\nPhase 4 must not create a second notification queue, ownership system or scene-host implementation.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 56. Phase 5 Handoff\r\n\r\nProvide Phase 5, **Awaken the Relics**, with frozen state/input contracts for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nJournal Clasp\r\nVoyage Compass\r\nFinale Mechanism\r\n```\r\n\r\nInclude:\r\n\r\n* state names;\r\n* input names;\r\n* transitions;\r\n* reduced pose;\r\n* fallback behavior;\r\n* production mount locations;\r\n* semantic scene labels;\r\n* load/failure behavior.\r\n\r\nPhase 5 should be able to install the real `.riv` binaries without redesigning the Player event system.\r\n\r\nAlso provide frozen Lottie and PageFlip lifecycle requirements.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 57. Mandatory Final Report\r\n\r\nAt completion report:\r\n\r\n## Project identity\r\n\r\n```text\r\nProgram: Project Lanternwake\r\nPhase: Phase 3\r\nPhase name: Unfurl the Tale\r\nFormal scope: Player Cinematic Integration, Persistent Progression Presentation, and Living Voyage Choreography\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Repository\r\n\r\n* branch;\r\n* starting commit;\r\n* ending commit;\r\n* upstream;\r\n* worktree safety;\r\n* concurrent work preserved;\r\n* files changed.\r\n\r\n## Requirement totals\r\n\r\n* Phase 3 Codex requirements;\r\n* Phase 3 OA requirements;\r\n* existing defects assigned to Phase 3;\r\n* total Phase 3 accepted requirements;\r\n* implemented;\r\n* validated;\r\n* externally asset-blocked;\r\n* environment-blocked;\r\n* superseded;\r\n* rejected;\r\n* unmapped.\r\n\r\nThe final unmapped count must be:\r\n\r\n```text\r\n0\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Architecture totals\r\n\r\n* persistent hosts;\r\n* event policies;\r\n* queue priorities;\r\n* replayable event types;\r\n* local enhancement contracts;\r\n* focus-restoration paths;\r\n* fallback contracts.\r\n\r\n## Event totals\r\n\r\n* event types implemented out of 17;\r\n* event/section cases passed out of 102;\r\n* full-mode cases;\r\n* gentle-mode cases;\r\n* reduced-mode cases;\r\n* viewport cases;\r\n* replay cases;\r\n* interruption cases.\r\n\r\n## Screen totals\r\n\r\n* Journal animations implemented;\r\n* pause/resume/undo animations implemented;\r\n* Quartermaster bridge animations implemented.\r\n\r\n## Test totals\r\n\r\n* unit tests added;\r\n* component tests added;\r\n* E2E tests added;\r\n* passed;\r\n* failed;\r\n* skipped;\r\n* blocked;\r\n* viewports tested;\r\n* motion states tested;\r\n* lifecycle cycles;\r\n* accessibility results;\r\n* performance results.\r\n\r\n## Evidence\r\n\r\nProvide exact evidence for:\r\n\r\n* chapter release from all six sections;\r\n* under-10-second completion;\r\n* refresh-safe replay;\r\n* replay with zero mutation;\r\n* two simultaneous events;\r\n* duplicate event suppression;\r\n* hidden PageFlip source exclusion;\r\n* changed-only route drawing;\r\n* explicit artifact handoff;\r\n* quest update specificity;\r\n* log wrapper ownership;\r\n* finale requirement specificity;\r\n* pause/resume/undo;\r\n* reduced semantic equality;\r\n* cleanup baseline.\r\n\r\n## Artifacts\r\n\r\n* design record;\r\n* implementation report;\r\n* validation report;\r\n* event coverage ledger;\r\n* matrix updates;\r\n* OA reconciliation updates;\r\n* roadmap updates;\r\n* test-plan updates;\r\n* visual checkpoint index;\r\n* chat synchronization;\r\n* Development_Docs synchronization.\r\n\r\n## Handoff\r\n\r\n* exact Phase 4 interfaces;\r\n* exact Phase 5 state contracts;\r\n* remaining blockers;\r\n* externally blocked assets;\r\n* no automatic start of Phase 4.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 58. Phase Completion Gate\r\n\r\nDo not declare **Unfurl the Tale** complete unless:\r\n\r\n1. The persistent Player ProgressionSceneHost is implemented.\r\n2. The host remains mounted across all six sections.\r\n3. All 17 event types have typed presentation policies.\r\n4. All 102 full-mode event/section cases pass.\r\n5. Chapter release works from every section.\r\n6. Chapter release completes in less than 10,000 ms.\r\n7. Replay survives refresh.\r\n8. Replay creates zero mutation.\r\n9. Every mandatory presentation has a readable fallback.\r\n10. Global and local presentations do not double-own acknowledgment.\r\n11. Journal opening paths converge on `JOURNAL_READY`.\r\n12. PageFlip lifecycle and focus remain stable.\r\n13. Map events affect only changed markers/routes.\r\n14. Artifact events use explicit handles.\r\n15. Quest events target exact quests/objectives.\r\n16. Log events avoid Motion/GSAP same-node conflicts.\r\n17. Finale events target exact requirements.\r\n18. Pause/resume/undo reconcile safely.\r\n19. Audio is label-synchronized after preflight.\r\n20. Full/gentle/reduced modes preserve semantic equality.\r\n21. Accessibility requirements pass.\r\n22. Zero accepted Phase 3 requirements remain unmapped.\r\n26. Governing documentation matches implemented reality.\r\n27. Concurrent work remains preserved.\r\n28. **Timestamp:** 2026-07-18T21:44:23.928Z

P3-R5 found a major route boundary: the six rich workspaces currently exist only under legacy `/tale/[campaignSlug]` through `PlayerExperience`, while the canonical active/resumed/paused/completed Player route uses `TallTaleJournalSession` with only chapters/current block plus map/artifact drawers. ## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-18T21:45:49.534Z

Two more cross-cutting findings sharpen the post-Phase-2 decision:

- The repository currently has two unbridged authoritative event stacks: the compatibility six-section `/tale` experience uses `Campaign`/`ProgressEvent`, while the canonical invitation/platform journal uses `TaleSession`/`TaleSessionEvent`. The canonical path has stronger membership revocation, but it advances reading sequence before any cinematic receipt and can collapse multiple transaction events into one live emission. Phase 3 must explicitly adapt the authoritative platform events; it cannot silently wire only the legacy campaign stack.
- Accessibility is not ready for the cinematic host: hidden canonical drawers are not inert, focus is not restored, opening overlays do not manage focus/background inertness, and chapter release currently forces Journal navigation. Audio mapping covers only 8 of 17 events, and the chapter ink Lottie is mounted without a semantic trigger.

These are read-only baseline findings. Their affected files will all be re-read after Phase 2 before architecture is frozen.

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-18T21:46:32.339Z

P3-R6 found several correctness risks that will shape the design gate: both SSE routes have a query-then-listen gap, both Player paths advance sequence/reading state before a presentation receipt, replay can currently abort a live event, and only chapter-release failure has durable recovery. The canonical loader can also overwrite/collapse pending events during pause/resume catch-up, while revocation handling does not yet terminate all polling/cached state. These are not animation polish issues; they are event-delivery and acknowledgment invariants that Phase 3 must fix against one chosen authority after Phase 2 lands.
