Phase 3 implementation must therefore wait; no finalized Phase 2 API was inferred.\n\n## Inspected\n\n- Phase 3 brief §§7, 9–12, 25–26, 30, 38–39.\n- `AnimationDirector`, `AnimationProvider`, registry, ownership, preflight, PageFlip boundary, Player progression handling, event domain, SSE, snapshot/replay, and viewed acknowledgment.\n- Key files:\n  - `src/animation/director/AnimationDirector.ts`\n  - `src/animation/director/AnimationProvider.tsx`\n  - `src/animation/director/scene-registry.ts`\n  - `src/animation/core/animation-types.ts`\n  - `src/animation/core/ownership.ts`\n  - `src/animation/core/target-preflight.ts`\n  - `src/components/player/PlayerExperience.tsx`\n  - `src/components/animation/PageFlipBook.tsx`\n  - `src/domain/story.ts`\n  - `src/domain/replay.ts`\n  - `src/domain/visibility.ts`\n  - `src/app/api/player/[campaignSlug]/events/route.ts`\n  - `src/app/api/player/[campaignSlug]/viewed/route.ts`\n  - `Development_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_1_Design_Record.md`\n\n## Current-state map\n\n- `AnimationProvider` creates one application-global `AnimationDirector` and owns the resolved motion policy.\n- `AnimationDirector` now provides truthful Phase 1 target preflight, ownership leases, semantic completion, typed receipts, fallback gating, interruption, and cleanup.\n- Its internal queue is only a FIFO promise tail. Numeric priority may cancel the active lower-priority scene, but it cannot reorder already queued work. Example: queued medium work still runs before a newly arrived critical scene after active playback is interrupted.\n- `PlayerExperience` currently provides:\n  - `eventChain` arrival-order serialization;\n  - in-memory ID deduplication through `seenEvents`;\n  - one `activeEvent`;\n  - one abort controller;\n  - temporary section switching for chapter release;\n  - acknowledgment only for `CHAPTER_RELEASED`;\n  - persisted replay only for the latest chapter release.\n- Live events are not sorted at the client by authoritative sequence; correctness relies on SSE delivery order.\n- The current Player root is also the compatibility scene root. It contains persistent UI, conditional section content, and temporary event props, so identical `data-scene-part` names can collide.\n- Registry host kinds are inconsistent: chapter release expects `\"player-progression\"` while other Player progression scenes expect `\"progression\"`.\n- Preflight scopes queries to the supplied root and rejects stale instance markers when present, but it does not require every accepted target to carry the current instance marker.\n- PageFlip removes source and scene-instance markers from visible clones but preserves scene-part/ownership attributes. Broad-root selection can therefore still encounter source/clone or section/global ambiguity.\n- The domain event union has 32 values, while Phase 3 §12 identifies a 17-event Player subset. A dedicated typed Player event subset is required.\n- Current replay and acknowledgment persistence are insufficient for the full Phase 3 replay/history contract.\n\n## Proposed architecture\n\n```text\nPlayerExperience\n├─ persistent ProgressionPresentationController\n├─ persistent ProgressionSceneHost\n│  ├─ backdrop/dimming\n│  ├─ primary ceremony\n│  ├─ event object\n│  ├─ readable summary\n│  ├─ controls\n│  ├─ live region\n│  └─ fallback\n└─ conditional section content\n   └─ optional section-local Phase 2 hosts\n```\n\nThe controller should own progression policy, ordering, deduplication, retries, replay arbitration, interruption, and reconciliation. The existing Director should remain the per-scene execution engine beneath it. Phase 3 must not reuse the Director’s FIFO as the authoritative progression queue.\n\nThe persistent host should consume the finalized Phase 2 registration/handle API and the existing provider-resolved motion policy. It should not instantiate a second Director or duplicate the authoritative snapshot/event store.\n\n### Progression-level interfaces\n\nThese shapes can be frozen independently of Phase 2 field details:\n\n- `PlayerSectionId`: derived from current `CompanionView`.\n- `ProgressionPresentationPriority`:\n  - `critical-authoritative`\n  - `mandatory-ceremony`\n  - `state-transition`\n  - `informational`\n  - `decorative`\n  - `manual-replay`\n- `ProgressionPresentationRequest`:\n  - unique request/attempt ID;\n  - immutable event ID and sequence;\n  - typed Player event type;\n  - scene name;\n  - typed Player-safe payload;\n  - source: live, reconnect, or replay;\n  - priority/policy identity;\n  - captured return section, focus, and scroll restoration;\n  - provider-resolved motion request, reconciled again at dispatch.\n- `ProgressionPresentationReceipt`:\n  - request/event/source identity;\n  - queue terminal status;\n  - optional Phase 1 `PresentationReceipt`;\n  - acknowledgment eligibility;\n  - duplicate/collision information;\n  - restoration and handoff results;\n  - exactly-once cleanup result.\n\nPhase 2 host handles, ownership scopes, external targets, and final-state handoffs should be consumed through an adapter rather than duplicated in Phase 3 types.\n\n## Queue policy\n\n- Live and reconnect events form one authoritative lane and are selected by ascending event sequence.\n- Priority controls preemption and resource arbitration; it should not silently reorder authoritative events.\n- Critical/live work may interrupt replay or decorative work at a declared semantic boundary.\n- Mandatory authoritative scenes never overlap.\n- Manual replay runs only when no accepted live/reconnect request is ready.\n- Section-local enhancements are outside the authoritative queue and can run only after explicit settled-state handoff and successful ownership acquisition.\n- Access revocation is a terminal control signal, not ordinary offline state.\n\n### Deduplication\n\nFor live/reconnect input, deduplicate by immutable event ID across queued, active, and terminal records.\n\nA duplicate must:\n\n- create no scene instance;\n- emit no audio;\n- create no acknowledgment;\n- leave ordering unchanged;\n- return a progression-level duplicate receipt.\n\nIf the same ID arrives with different sequence, type, or payload identity, classify it as an integrity collision, not a normal duplicate.\n\nReplay uses the same event identity but a new request and scene instance. Repeated replay-button activation should be request-debounced, not confused with live-event deduplication.\n\nA failed mandatory automatic attempt remains explicitly retryable. Retry uses the stored immutable envelope through a controller method; it does not masquerade as a newly received event.\n\n## State machine\n\n```text\nqueued\n  → preparing\n  → presenting\n  → normalizing\n  → terminal\n\nterminal:\n  completed\n  presented-fallback\n  skipped-readable\n  duplicate\n  interrupted\n  failed-retryable\n  cancelled\n  revoked\n```\n\nController-wide modes should separately track `online`, `offline-paused`, `reconciling`, and `revoked`.\n\nEvery accepted request receives exactly one terminal progression receipt. Director interruption, unmount, motion change, offline transition, navigation, asset failure, timeout, and ownership rejection all converge through the same normalization/finalization path.\n\nAcknowledgment eligibility is emitted only when:\n\n- source is live or reconnect, never replay;\n- the Phase 1 receipt permits acknowledgment;\n- readable final state and cleanup requirements passed.\n\nThe data layer persists acknowledgment idempotently; the host does not become the business-state store.\n\n## Host and target rules\n\n- Required authoritative targets must live inside the active global scene-instance root.\n- The Director should receive the Phase 2 active instance root, never the broad Player root.\n- Section targets and PageFlip clones must not be required for global success.\n- External section target handles may be used only for optional handoff/local enhancement.\n- Each layer needs one declared runtime owner. Structural React wrappers, Motion layout wrappers, and GSAP/Rive/Lottie actor children should remain distinct.\n- Inactive host layers use `pointer-events: none`/inert semantics and cannot permanently block section controls.\n- Focus trapping occurs only for policy-declared modal ceremonies.\n- Section navigation is never automatic. Capture current section, focus, and scroll; restore them after completion/cancellation.\n- Local enhancement owns no acknowledgment, replay receipt, queue, fallback, or duplicate global scene.\n\n## Reconnect/replay model\n\nKeep separate cursors for:\n\n- authoritative snapshot sequence;\n- received event sequence;\n- presentation terminal state;\n- acknowledgment state.\n\nAfter reconnect:\n\n1. fetch authoritative state and Player-safe history;\n2. merge by immutable ID;\n3. detect collisions/stale entries;\n4. sort unseen accepted events by sequence;\n5. show one bounded readable reconciliation summary;\n6. queue mandatory events;\n7. acknowledge only valid presentations.\n\nDo not blindly resume a timeline against reconciled DOM. Per policy, restart, resume from a supported semantic checkpoint, or settle to readable final state.\n\nReplay always uses persisted Player-safe history, current motion policy, a new scene instance, no acknowledgment, no progression mutation, and section/focus restoration. Live work may preempt replay at a semantic boundary; interrupted replay remains available.\n\n## Phase 2 dependencies that must remain unresolved\n\nImplementation must wait for Phase 2 to freeze:\n\n- host ID/kind/instance typing and registration lifecycle;\n- active instance-root acquisition;\n- host-local Director dispatch and concurrency model;\n- external target-handle validity and lifetime;\n- ownership scope/claim result semantics;\n- final-state handoff shape and acknowledgment;\n- PageFlip source/clone exposure contract;\n- unregister/unmount cleanup behavior;\n- canonical progression host kind;\n- request-scoped semantic-label delivery for synchronized audio.\n\nThe last point matters because current Director labels are exposed through one global snapshot and final receipts; Phase 3 needs instance-scoped label delivery without cross-scene races.\n\n## Primary risks\n\n- Using the Director FIFO as the Phase 3 queue.\n- Broad-root target collisions between global props, current sections, and PageFlip content.\n- Reordering authoritative events by presentation priority.\n- Treating replay as an inbound duplicate.\n- Consuming one-shot state after failed/invisible/interrupted presentation.\n- Allowing automatic retry storms.\n- Global `director.cancel()` affecting an unrelated host.\n- Motion-mode interruption without controller-driven restart/finalization.\n- Loose `Record<string, unknown>` event payloads.\n- Conflating the 32-event domain union with the 17-event Player policy subset.\n- Revocation repeatedly reconnecting as if merely offline.\n- Audio replaying for duplicate or failed preflight.\n\n## Required focused tests\n\n- Pure queue ordering, source arbitration, duplicate/collision handling, bounded retry, and replay preemption.\n- Out-of-order reconnect batches sorted by sequence.\n- Duplicate ID creates no instance/audio/acknowledgment.\n- Failed mandatory presentation remains retryable and does not consume one-shot state.\n- Persistent host survives every section switch and rerender.\n- Two hosts with identical part names cannot cross-select.\n- Hidden PageFlip source and unintended clones cannot qualify.\n- Rejected ownership blocks playback and releases cleanly.\n- Global-to-local handoff produces no snapback or duplicate cinematic.\n- Every interruption in §39 yields one receipt, stable readable state, no detached write, no leaked host/claim/audio, and replay availability.\n- Replay after refresh/remount/motion change/skip/interruption/reconnect causes no command, progression, presence, or viewed mutation.\n- Revocation terminates the queue and scrubs private cached payloads.\n- Modal versus non-modal focus behavior and section scroll restoration.\n\nConfidence: high on current-state findings and scheduler/host separation; medium on exact integration signatures until Phase 2 lands. No files were edited, no runtime/database/shared-resource operations were performed, and no tests were run in this read-only lane."}},{"agent_name":"/root/p3_r3_event_bridge","agent_status":{"completed":"P3-R3 read-only event-bridge map is complete against the working tree at `19b87c3` as of 2026-07-18 17:45 ET. No files, database, runtime, or Git state were changed.\n\n## Executive result\n\nAll 17 Phase 3 event families have a current legacy `Campaign` producer and Player scene mapping. However, only `CHAPTER_RELEASED` currently has a truthful persisted presentation acknowledgment and replay source.\n\nThree architectural blockers are confirmed:\n\n1. The visible Quartermaster UI bypasses the hardened command path.\n2. Snapshot synchronization is being reported as Player acknowledgment for the other event families.\n3. The canonical Tall Tale invitation/playthrough runtime and the six-section cinematic `Campaign` runtime are separate event stacks with no adapter between them.\n\n## Current end-to-end flow\n\n### Legacy six-section Player experience\n\n1. Quartermaster selects one of the 17-producing actions in [Quartermaster.tsx](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/src/components/gm/Quartermaster.tsx:80).\n2. Confirmation starts a Captain-side animation whose `operation` POSTs `/api/gm/action`, not `/api/gm/commands`, in [Quartermaster.tsx](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/src/components/gm/Quartermaster.tsx:332).\n3. [action/route.ts](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/src/app/api/gm/action/route.ts:5) validates GM session, CSRF, and `gmActionSchema`, then calls `executeProgressionAction`.\n4. [progression.ts](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/src/server/progression.ts:93) transactionally:\n\n   - saves pre-action state for undo;\n   - mutates authoritative campaign/domain rows;\n   - increments `Campaign.currentSequence`;\n   - inserts `ProgressEvent`;\n   - inserts `CampaignSnapshot`;\n   - inserts `AdminAuditLog`.\n\n5. After commit, `toClientEvent` applies the per-event allowlist in [visibility.ts](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/src/domain/visibility.ts:11), then `publishCampaignEvent` emits through the process-local `EventEmitter` in [events.ts](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/src/lib/events.ts:9).\n6. [events/route.ts](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/src/app/api/player/[campaignSlug]/events/route.ts:8) authenticates `PlayerAccess`, replays persisted rows after a sequence, then subscribes to the in-process bus.\n7. [PlayerExperience.tsx](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/src/components/player/PlayerExperience.tsx:507) receives SSE, deduplicates with an in-memory set, serializes arrival through `eventChain`, and maps the 17 types to scenes through `sceneByEvent` at line 44.\n8. For `CHAPTER_RELEASED`, it reloads the persisted safe snapshot, waits for the presentation receipt, and POSTs `/viewed` only when the receipt permits acknowledgment.\n9. For the other 16 types, the director receipt is discarded: there is no persisted presentation acknowledgment, failure recovery, or replay history.\n\nTruth boundary: committed state/event/audit rows prove persistence. The Quartermaster toast and `delivery: \"PUBLISHED\"` do not prove that a Player received or presented an event.\n\n### Hardened command path, currently not used by the UI\n\n`/api/gm/commands` and `executeAdminCommand` add expected-sequence conflicts, idempotency, `CommandExecution`, correlation IDs, failed audit rows, and persisted results in [admin-command.ts](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/src/server/admin-command.ts:156). The E2E tests exercise this API directly in [command-center.spec.ts](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/tests/e2e/command-center.spec.ts:42).\n\nThe production Quartermaster buttons still use `/api/gm/action`, so they receive none of those protections. `commandCapability()` is defined but used only by its unit test; neither command route enforces it.\n\n## Event-family coverage\n\n| Phase 3 family | Current producer | Player scene | Persistence | Ack / replay |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| `CHAPTER_RELEASED` | `RELEASE_CHAPTER` | `chapter-release` | State + event + snapshot + audit | Persisted per campaign/device/event; latest release replay only |\n| `CHAPTER_SOLVED` | `MARK_SOLVED` | `mark-solved` | Yes | Receipt discarded; no replay |\n| Artifact award / silhouette / connection | `AWARD_ARTIFACT`, `REVEAL_ARTIFACT_SILHOUETTE`, `CONNECT_ARTIFACTS` | `artifact-award`, `artifact-connection` | Yes | No persisted presentation ack/replay |\n| Map location / route | `REVEAL_MAP`, `REVEAL_ROUTE` | `map-reveal`, `route-draw` | Yes | No persisted presentation ack/replay |\n| Quest discovery / update / completion | `DISCOVER_SIDE_QUEST`, `UPDATE_SIDE_QUEST`, `COMPLETE_SIDE_QUEST` | `quest-discovery`, `quest-complete` | Yes | No persisted presentation ack/replay |\n| Annotation / log entry | `ADD_JOURNAL_ANNOTATION`, `ADD_LOG_ENTRY` | `log-entry` | Yes | No persisted presentation ack/replay |\n| Finale tease / requirement | `TEASE_FINALE`, `UPDATE_FINALE_REQUIREMENT` | `finale-tease`, `finale-requirement` | Yes | No persisted presentation ack/replay |\n| Pause / resume / undo | `PAUSE`, `RESUME`, `UNDO_LAST` | `pause`, `resume`, `undo` | Yes | No persisted presentation ack/replay |\n\nThe mapping is visually exhaustive, but it is a `Partial<Record<ProgressEventType, ...>>`, not the Phase 3 exhaustive typed policy that must fail when a required event lacks a declaration.\n\n## Phase 3 section comparison\n\n### Section 22 — Quartermaster bridge\n\nSupported:\n\n- real server operation and pending UI;\n- transactional authoritative event creation;\n- Player-safe SSE;\n- Captain receives event ID and sequence;\n- process-local live publication plus database catch-up.\n\nMissing or unsafe:\n\n- visible UI does not perform command-specific `/preview` preflight;\n- visible UI has no expected sequence, idempotency key, target/reason, or correlation ID;\n- its `AdminAuditLog.correlationId` is normally null;\n- no capability enforcement;\n- only chapter release acknowledges after a Player receipt;\n- command and Player presentations cannot be correlated end-to-end;\n- Captain “Player synchronized” diagnostics are not presentation truth.\n\nThere is also a serious post-commit failure window: `executeProgressionAction` commits, publishes the Player event, and only then builds the response snapshot. If snapshot/response work fails, the Captain can receive an error while the Player has already received real success. The hardened path likewise marks `CommandExecution` only after the progression transaction/publication, so a crash or update failure can leave a committed Player event with a failed or permanently running Captain receipt.\n\n### Section 26 / 37 — Replay\n\nCurrent replay is limited to the latest `CHAPTER_RELEASED` through `PublicSnapshot.latestChapterReleasePresentation`. It correctly reconstructs safe content from authoritative state and does not reuse the raw narrative SSE payload.\n\nMissing:\n\n- Player-facing persisted history for the other replayable families;\n- replay after more than the latest chapter release;\n- immutable payload/version projection for every family;\n- source distinction in the transport (`live`, `reconnect`, `replay`);\n- the full 12-step protocol for each replayable scene;\n- persisted proof that replay generated no viewed receipt.\n\n### Section 30 — Offline and reconnect\n\nMissing:\n\n- unseen event calculation from persisted per-event presentation receipts;\n- readable multi-event reconciliation summary;\n- mandatory-event queue after reconnect;\n- explicit gap detection;\n- stale-event policy;\n- legacy Player access-revocation signal;\n- retry after a failed non-chapter presentation.\n\n`PlayerExperience` opens SSE with `after = snapshot.sequence`. Consequently, events already represented in the initial snapshot are treated as synchronized and never automatically presented, except for the special latest-chapter recovery.\n\n### Sections 38–39 — one-shot and interruption\n\nCurrent scopes are inconsistent:\n\n- `seenEvents` and automatic-attempt sets: component mount only;\n- journal introduction: browser tab/session storage per campaign;\n- chapter ceremony: campaign + local device + event ID;\n- ordinary viewed content: `PlayerAccess` + content type/key;\n- canonical journal `lastEventSequence`: profile preferences or local storage.\n\nA non-chapter event is inserted into `seenEvents` before presentation. If presentation fails, the duplicate is suppressed for the rest of that mount. There is no duplicate receipt, retry state, or persisted failure receipt.\n\nOnly chapter presentation failures are surfaced. Other interruption outcomes are swallowed after the director returns.\n\n## Missing request/receipt envelope fields\n\n`ClientProgressEvent` currently contains only:\n\n```text\nid, type, sequence, payload, releaseAt\n```\n\nThe bridge still needs a typed request/envelope carrying or resolving:\n\n- `source: live | reconnect | replay`;\n- correlation/command execution ID;\n- event payload/schema version;\n- actor/source identity class;\n- `reversesEventId` and supersession identity;\n- priority, mandatory status, replay policy, and one-shot scope;\n- return section/current section;\n- requested motion policy;\n- authoritative snapshot/state version;\n- presentation attempt/scene instance linkage.\n\n`PresentationReceipt` has scene instance, host, outcome, labels, target report, acknowledgment eligibility, and cleanup, but lacks event sequence/correlation and is only held in memory. Presentation telemetry is also an in-memory capped diagnostic recorder, not a durable Player receipt.\n\n`STATE_REVERTED` is especially under-specified: persistence knows `reversesEventId`, but Player serialization exposes only `reversedType`, so it cannot render the required exact previous/current identities.\n\n## Transport and race risks\n\n- Both SSE routes query missed rows and only then subscribe to the in-process bus. An event committed/published in that interval is missed until a later reconnect.\n- Live fan-out is process-local; another application instance will not receive the event.\n- `eventChain` preserves arrival order, not authoritative sequence order; there is no buffer for gaps/out-of-order arrival.\n- Duplicate IDs are silently dropped rather than producing the required duplicate receipt.\n- `latestSequence` advances on receipt before successful presentation.\n- Presence posts `acknowledgedSequence: snapshot.sequence` in [PlayerExperience.tsx](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/src/components/player/PlayerExperience.tsx:536). GM status therefore can report zero lag even when a scene failed or was never attempted.\n- `/viewed` accepts event presentation acknowledgments only for `CHAPTER_RELEASED` in [viewed/route.ts](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/src/app/api/player/[campaignSlug]/viewed/route.ts:19).\n- Legacy SSE validates access only when the stream opens and has no terminal revocation mechanism.\n\n## Canonical Tall Tale platform split\n\nThe canonical invitation/runtime stack uses:\n\n```text\nInvitation\n-> PlayerProfile\n-> PlaythroughMembership\n-> TaleSession\n-> TaleSessionEvent\n-> /api/play/sessions/[sessionId]/events\n-> TallTaleJournalSession\n```\n\nThis stack has hashed invitation credentials, player identity, membership checks, and identity revocation polling. It is not connected to `Campaign`, `ProgressEvent`, `PlayerAccess`, or `PlayerExperience`.\n\nIts persisted `TaleSessionEvent` envelope is richer—published version, block, source, idempotency key, sequence, and optional correlation—but the Player SSE strips it to ID/type/sequence/time. The Player state API exposes only the latest 40 events and removes correlation/version/idempotency details.\n\nAdditional confirmed gaps:\n\n- `captainSessionAction` can persist several events in one transaction but emits only the returned event.\n- `TallTaleJournalSession` initializes `processedSequence` to the current snapshot sequence and persists `lastEventSequence` without a cinematic receipt.\n- concurrent catch-up loads can collapse several events into one latest pending notice;\n- canonical event vocabulary (`blockEntered`, `artifactGranted`, `sessionPaused`, etc.) has no declared projection to the Phase 3 17-event policy;\n- no per-event viewed/presentation receipt or cinematic replay model exists.\n\nPhase 3 therefore needs an explicit architecture decision: either adapt authoritative `TaleSessionEvent` into the 17 presentation families and mount the six-section host in the canonical Player runtime, or formally retain the legacy campaign stack and document why it remains authoritative. Silently implementing only the legacy bridge would leave real invitation-based Players outside Phase 3.\n\n## Focused test recommendations\n\n1. Actual Quartermaster UI test proving it uses the hardened correlated/idempotent path for all 17 producers.\n2. Post-commit failure injection proving Captain failure can never coexist with a committed/published Player success.\n3. Transaction evidence test for state mutation + event + audit + correlation, followed separately by Player receipt.\n4. Ordered reconnect test with gaps, stale events, duplicate IDs, and an event in the replay-query/subscription race window.\n5. All 17 events from all six sections, asserting exactly one receipt and acknowledgment only after a valid outcome.\n6. Failed/aborted/invisible presentation test proving the one-shot key remains unconsumed.\n7. Generalized persisted receipt tests keyed by player/access context + device + event + scene attempt.\n8. Replay protocol for every replayable policy, asserting zero mutation routes and unchanged event/audit/receipt counts.\n9. Presence test proving snapshot synchronization cannot advance presentation acknowledgment.\n10. Privacy table test for every event payload, including undo identities and canonical `TaleSessionEvent`.\n11. Mid-stream revocation tests for both identity membership and legacy access.\n12. Canonical adapter test proving every transaction-created TaleSession event reaches the presentation queue, including multi-event transactions.\n\nConfidence is high for the static producer, persistence, transport, and client-flow map. Runtime behavior was not exercised because this lane was explicitly read-only with no server or database ownership. The four relevant Player/UI files were already modified by concurrent Phase work, so the coordinator should recheck these findings after Phase 2 finishes before granting write ownership.\n\n"}},{"agent_name":"/root/p3_r4_journal","agent_status":{"completed":"P3-R4 Journal lane complete — read-only; no files changed or runtime resources used.\n\n## Canonical route and no-duplicate integration\n\nConfirmed evidence:\n\n- `/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/journal` mounts `TallTaleJournalSession` with durable Player identity: `src/app/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/journal/page.tsx:1-9`.\n- The legacy token session mounts the same component: `src/app/play/[taleSlug]/session/[sessionId]/page.tsx:1-4`.\n- The old archive route redirects to that journal: `src/app/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/archive/page.tsx:1-7`.\n- `/tale/[campaignSlug]` separately mounts `PlayerExperience`: `src/app/tale/[campaignSlug]/page.tsx:1-10`.\n- Canonical/compatibility status is explicit in `docs/journal-system.md:5-7`, `docs/tall-tale-platform.md:7-10`, `docs/architecture.md:40`, and `Development_Docs/Canonical_Player_Journal_Implementation_Record.md:43-46,64`.\n\nRecommended integration:\n\n1. Keep `/player/playthroughs/[id]/journal` as the canonical active/resumed/paused/completed gameplay route.\n2. selected source/scene identity attributes are stripped from clones.\n- One queued programmatic destination is retained while flipping.\n- Page and internal focus survive full/gentle/reduced runtime recreation.\n- Canonical reading position is separate from progression.\n- Historical pages remain pinned, immutable and action-disabled.\n\nMaterial gaps:\n\n- The physical sequence lacks distinct camera approach, pre-latch shadow grounding, clasp wake, page-stack compression, wax-fragment settle, PageFlip-ready, interface-ready and objective-ready checkpoints.\n- `book-camera` has no actual phase movement; `book-shadow` is not a declared actor.\n- The Journal Clasp contract is unmounted, has no inputs, lacks `interrupted`, and current visuals are only handwritten CSS latch pieces. Do not claim live Rive.\n- `JOURNAL_READY` does not wait for PageFlip initialization or fallback readiness. PageFlip mounts behind the closed cover immediately, and the cover-to-clone transition has no readiness receipt or geometry handshake.\n- General abort in `TallTaleJournalSession.startOpening` returns to `ENTRY_IDLE`; Phase 3 requires surviving interruption paths to converge on `JOURNAL_READY`.\n- `PlayerExperience` and `TallTaleJournalSession` duplicate opening orchestration and currently differ in failure/abort/replay behavior.\n- Canonical `TallTaleJournalSession` has no manual full or abbreviated replay controls.\n- Historical mode still starts SSE and displays “Captain channel connected,” creating the live-event expectation the brief forbids.\n- No opening focus capture/transfer exists. Focus can remain on the now-hidden Open button.\n- Canonical background tools/PageFlip controls are not made inert while the full-screen opening is active.\n- Closed drawers use `aria-hidden` without `inert`, leaving a potential hidden-focus path.\n- Full PageFlip turns move focus from the initiating control to the visible page, while reduced turns retain control focus; semantic focus behavior differs by mode.\n- Current PageFlip lifecycle is only `folding | flipping | read` plus `flip`. It does not emit `turn-start`, `turn-commit`, `turn-settle`, `turn-cancel`, or `turn-failed`.\n- Page-turn audio is triggered when state becomes `flipping` but labeled `\"page-turn-complete\"`, so timing and semantics disagree.\n- No queued-turn indicator, active chapter-tab state, committed tab handoff, orientation-settle label, or complete runtime-failure transition exists.\n- Page-edge hover lift is absent; midpoint shadow is partial; drag affordance is library-default only.\n- Chapter ribbon, bookmark movement, current-objective page highlight, hint foldout, read/unread ink, historical watermark entrance and locked-page shimmer are absent. Completion stamp is static.\n- Illustration transformation begins on clone/content mount, not declared visible-page entry.\n- Compatibility annotations render as static pages, discard their `unseen` flag in the page model, and have no visible-clone target. `JOURNAL_ANNOTATION_ADDED` currently shares the generic log overlay with no readable summary/destination action or Journal-local ink.\n- The canonical Tall Tale event taxonomy is different from the compatibility shell’s 17 `ClientProgressEvent` types; it must not be copied or guessed.\n\n## Required semantic stages and targets\n\nFreeze these in the Phase 3 design record before implementation:\n\n| Semantic stage | Required target/evidence |\n|---|---|\n| Entry activated | Opening host, readable heading/status, captured trigger/focus |\n| Camera approach | `book-camera`, stable responsive geometry |\n| Shadow grounding | `book-shadow`, settled before clasp travel |\n| Clasp wake | decorative `journal-clasp`, semantic state `awake` |\n| Latch release | latch wrapper, not unrelated cover descendants |\n| Cover opening | `front-cover`, weighted open pose |\n| Page-stack compression | left/right stacks with explicit CSS ownership |\n| Sealed-page reveal | `sealed-page` |\n| Seal pressure/fracture | `wax-seal`, cracks and fragments |\n| Wax-fragment settle | bounded fragment completion |\n| Book settle | camera/open-book geometry |\n| PageFlip handoff | Phase-2-final visible runtime reports interactive or static fallback; current page preserved |\n| Interface reveal | persistent controls reachable |\n| Objective reveal | objective note reachable/readable |\n| Journal ready | page index, focus target, controls, announcement and cleanup verified |\n\nOpening profiles should be data-driven:\n\n- First/full and manual-full replay traverse the complete sequence.\n- Reentry/abbreviated replay uses a 350–650 ms atmosphere/book-settle path.\n- Completed archive uses a quiet archival profile, no live-channel language, and preserves replay/history.\n- Reduced mode applies the same semantic outcomes directly without physical travel.\n- Skip, mode change, recoverable interruption and PageFlip failure apply a final readable pose, preserve page index, restore deliberate focus and end at `JOURNAL_READY`.\n- Route unmount only cleans up; it must not write to detached DOM.\n\nUse a semantic `locked | awake | opening | open | interrupted` clasp adapter driving SVG/CSS now. no six-section/event-store duplication.\n- Shared book-shell owner: `PhysicalJournalBook.tsx` and a possible semantic CSS clasp adapter.\n- Phase 2 → Phase 3 serialized handoff owner: `PageFlipBook.tsx` and its tests.\n- Persistent-host owner: `PlayerExperience.tsx`; consume Journal callbacks only.\n- Annotation-local owner: `JournalWorkspace.tsx`, page model/render targets and annotation tests; no global acknowledgment.\n- Shared CSS owner: one serialized writer for `player.css`/`tall-tale.css`.\n- Scene owner: keep `journal-open` and GSAP curl scenes deprecated; Findings are provisional until Phase 2 finishes; no files, runtime, database, or Git state were changed.\n\n## Canonical-route finding\n\nThe repository currently has two Player experiences:\n\n- `/tale/[campaignSlug]` renders `PlayerExperience` and contains all six Phase 3 sections plus the legacy 17-event taxonomy.\n- `/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/journal` renders `TallTaleJournalSession`.\n- `/play/[taleSlug]/session/[sessionId]` renders the same `TallTaleJournalSession`.\n- Completed archives redirect to that canonical journal route.\n- `PlayerVoyageRoom` sends launched and completed modern playthroughs toward the journal runtime.\n\nThis supports the governing architecture statement that `TallTaleJournalSession` is the canonical active/resumed/paused/completed Tall Tale experience. However, `/tale` remains production-reachable and is exercised by the Quartermaster/Campaign acceptance suite, so it should be treated as a compatibility surface, not deleted or assumed obsolete.\n\nImplementing the six Phase 3 surfaces only inside `PlayerExperience` would miss the canonical Player journey. The safe direction is to extend the canonical journal’s Player-safe projection and reuse normalized surface/event contracts across both routes. Do not create a second modern Player application or another `/player/.../companion` route.\n\nExact evidence:\n\n- `src/app/tale/[campaignSlug]/page.tsx` → `PlayerExperience`\n- `src/app/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/journal/page.tsx` → `TallTaleJournalSession`\n- `src/app/play/[taleSlug]/session/[sessionId]/page.tsx` → `TallTaleJournalSession`\n- `src/app/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/archive/page.tsx` → journal redirect\n- `src/components/platform/PlayerVoyageRoom.tsx:63-69` → live/completed journal handoff\n- `docs/architecture.md:40-44` → canonical-journal invariant\n\n## Current capability and target inventory\n\n| Surface/events | Current legacy capability and ownership | Current target/scene hooks | Confirmed gaps |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Voyage Chart: `MAP_LOCATION_REVEALED`, `MAP_ROUTE_REVEALED` | `VoyageChart` renders `PublicSnapshot.mapLocations/mapRoutes`; owns local pan/zoom state. Projection originates in `buildPublicSnapshot`. | `VoyageChart.tsx:65-102`: generic `route-path`, index-selected `map-marker-new`, `map-fog`, `ship-token`, one generic route-motion path. `story.scene.ts:239-299`: `mapRevealScene`, `routeDrawScene`, future `markerStampScene` and `shipCourseScene`. | React keys are not DOM target identities. Every route shares `route-path`; “new marker” is merely the last array item. No event-key handle, region-bound fog handle, route-owned motion path, marker details action, or local handoff. A location reveal also draws a generic route. |\n| Treasure Altar: award, silhouette, connection | `TreasureAltar` renders `PublicSnapshot.artifacts`; `ArtifactInspection` owns modal/focus state via `PlayerExperience`. | `TreasureAltar.tsx:33-95`: generic light, every connection as `artifact-connection-path`, first visible artifact as `artifact-slot-target`, permanent `artifact-reveal`. `story.scene.ts:301-375`: award FLIP, connection draw, future inspection scene. | Destination selection is array index/first match, not payload key. Connection path has no connection identity. Award source/destination are queried with broad `querySelector`. There is no actual global-to-Altar handoff. |\n| Artifact inspection | Accessible dialog and focus restoration exist. Motion opens/closes and shared `layoutId` is present. | `ArtifactInspection.tsx:47-107`: `motion.section`, `motion.div`, generic `artifact-reveal`, and `artifact-engraving`. Registry marks `artifact-inspection` future-only. | No production scene caller. The `motion.div` carrying shared layout is also marked `data-gsap-owned`, contrary to the required wrapper split. No artifact-key DOM/handle contract or return-to-slot animation receipt. |\n| Side quests: discovered, updated, completed | `SideQuestLedger` projects `PublicSnapshot.sideQuests` into a separate `PageFlipBook`; owns filter and book ref. | `SideQuestLedger.tsx:50-99`: last filtered quest becomes `quest-note-new`; generic `red-thread`; any completion page may contain generic `quest-stamp`. `story.scene.ts:378-410`: discovery and complete scenes. | No exact quest/objective handle. `SIDE_QUEST_UPDATED` maps to `quest-discovery`, violating the requirement not to reuse discovery choreography blindly. No changed-objective target exists. Filter/order changes can select the wrong quest. Targets inside PageFlip source/clone boundaries cannot be finalized until Phase 2. |\n| Journal annotation | Legacy annotation pages have stable model IDs such as `chapter-{ordinal}-annotation-{key}` in `src/animation/journal/page-model.ts:97-107`. | `JOURNAL_ANNOTATION_ADDED` currently maps to global `log-entry`; `JournalWorkspace` page DOM exposes no annotation-key scene target. | No visible-page external handle, automatic turn/handoff, new-annotation ink child, or section-local scene. Page IDs are not exposed as stable visible-clone target handles. |\n| Ship’s Log: `PLAYER_LOG_ENTRY_ADDED` | `ShipsLog` groups the event-derived `snapshot.log`; owns filter state and destination navigation. `eventToLogEntry` keeps a safe target key. | `ShipsLog.tsx:53-89`: Motion day/row layout; last row per day becomes `log-entry-new`; child may become `log-symbol-new`. `story.scene.ts:413-430`: log scene. | The scene animates transform on `motion.li` because the same node is also `data-gsap-owned`, directly violating Section 19. With descending events, “last per day” is generally the oldest, and multiple days create duplicate `log-entry-new` targets. No event-ID attribute, fresh-ink child wrapper, date-stamp target, or exact route-back target. |\n| Finale: teased, requirement updated | `FinaleChamber` renders `snapshot.finale`, requirements, static mechanism, Lottie fog, and the Phase 5 Rive fallback component. | `FinaleChamber.tsx:40-77`: generic rings/light path; requirement rows have React keys only. `story.scene.ts:433-459`: tease and generic requirement draw. | No requirement-key socket/path handle; updates cannot animate only the changed requirement. `RiveStatefulObject` is mounted without `signal`, resolved motion policy, or reduced semantic pose, so the supported state interface is not driven. READY versus COMPLETE and unlock controls are absent. |\n| Pause, resume, undo | Legacy server persists campaign status and saved-state undo. `PlayerExperience` maps all three to scenes. | Pause/resume animate the global lantern; undo conditionally mounts a generic `undo-mark`. `command.scene.ts:88-90`. | No readable global status card, persisted paused styling/control reconciliation, state-version dedupe, previous-versus-current summary, or affected-object identity. Resume can only animate the lantern and has no cue mapping. |\n| Global presentation props | `PlayerProgressionProp` mounts generic props beneath the whole `PlayerExperience` root. | `PlayerExperience.tsx:1066-1138` provides map/artifact/quest/log/finale target shapes. | Props are decorative `aria-hidden`, contain no authoritative names/summaries/actions, and carry no event ID or entity key. They cannot satisfy readable global presentation or destination-action requirements. |\n\n## Critical target-integrity defect\n\n`PlayerExperience` places `data-scene-host-id=\"player-progression-host\"` on the entire `<main>`, including the currently mounted section. It then mounts `PlayerProgressionProp` before calling the director.\n\nConsequently, a relevant section commonly exposes both the temporary global target and permanent local targets:\n\n- Chart: global marker/path plus section marker/all route paths.\n- Altar: global artifact source/slot plus section source/slot.\n- Quest: global note/stamp plus PageFlip targets.\n- Log: global row plus section rows.\n- Finale: global rings/path plus section rings/path.\n\nScene target cardinality defaults to `max: 1` in `scene-registry.ts:57-84`, so Phase 1 preflight should reject these duplicates. Existing acceptance tests only assert that the refreshed settled state appears; they do not prove the cinematic target succeeded. Phase 2’s isolated host/local-handle model is therefore a hard prerequisite.\n\nOther orchestration gaps visible in `PlayerExperience.playEvent`:\n\n- Snapshot reconciliation occurs after the automatic scene, so target-specific content may not exist during presentation.\n- Only chapter release invokes viewed acknowledgment; non-chapter receipts do not post an acknowledgment.\n- Relevant local components receive no `activeEvent` or explicit local handoff.\n- `SIDE_QUEST_UPDATED` incorrectly uses `quest-discovery`.\n- `JOURNAL_ANNOTATION_ADDED` is treated as a generic log prop.\n\n## Missing canonical-journal projections\n\n`PlayerJournalProjection` currently contains only:\n\n- mode;\n- current chapter/block IDs;\n- released chapters and blocks.\n\n`TallTaleJournalSession` offers drawers for chapters, map pages, artifact pages, and messages, but it does not project or render the six Phase 3 workspace models:\n\n- chart locations/routes/fog/current ship position;\n- artifact collection, silhouettes, connections, and altar placement;\n- side quests/objectives/rewards;\n- persistent Ship’s Log;\n- finale state/requirements;\n- per-section unseen counts.\n\nAdditional canonical gaps:\n\n- `PlayerJournalProjection` has no replayable presentation history.\n- `TallTaleJournalSession.ProgressionEvent` contains only ID/type/sequence/time.\n- The SSE route strips `blockId` and sanitized payload, so an event-specific target cannot be resolved from the live envelope.\n- The full HTTP session response already carries player-safe recent event payloads and inventory in `src/tall-tale/progression.ts:693-733`, but `TallTaleJournalSession.SessionState` neither types nor consumes them.\n- Canonical event names are `artifactGranted`, `sessionPaused`, `sessionResumed`, `captainRollback`, `sessionCompleted`, `blockEntered`, etc. They do not directly provide the legacy 17-event map/quest/log/finale taxonomy.\n- Canonical map/artifact block pages are ordinary PageFlip content, without event-specific target handles.\n- Side quests, Ship’s Log, finale requirement progress, map routes, silhouette/connection events, and journal annotation events have no equivalent canonical projection yet.\n\nA normalized Player-safe journey projection/event adapter must be frozen before surface writers begin. Whether the canonical Tall Tale engine must emit all 17 semantic events or derive some from block/inventory transitions remains unresolved and should not be guessed.\n\n## Legacy payload blockers\n\nMost entity events carry useful stable keys, but two presentation requirements remain blocked by current payload timing/content:\n\n- `FINALE_REQUIREMENT_UPDATED` exposes only the requirement key. Label/current/target are available only after loading the new snapshot.\n- Stored `STATE_REVERTED` has `reversedEventId` and `reversedType`, but the public sanitizer exposes only `reversedType`. It cannot produce the required previous/current summary or affected object identities.\n- `PLAYER_LOG_ENTRY_ADDED.payload.key` differs from the projected log row key, which is the progress-event ID. The event ID should be the stable row handle.\n\n## Proposed future writer boundaries\n\nAll paths must be reverified after Phase 2 lands.\n\n- Canonical projection/event adapter owner, single writer:\n\n  - `src/tall-tale/journal-contract.ts`\n  - `src/tall-tale/progression.ts`\n  - `src/app/api/play/sessions/[sessionId]/events/route.ts`\n  - canonical projection-focused tests\n\n  This owner defines one normalized Player journey projection and safe event envelope; no UI lane invents its own adapter.\n\n- Canonical integration owner, single writer:\n\n  - `src/components/player/journal/TallTaleJournalSession.tsx`\n  - route/focus/section integration tests\n\n  It integrates reusable surface components into the canonical journal without creating a duplicate application.\n\n- Map surface writer:\n\n  - `src/components/player/workspace/VoyageChart.tsx`\n  - map-only local scene modules/tests\n  - no central host, queue, journal, registry, or global CSS edits\n\n- Artifact surface writer:\n\n  - `TreasureAltar.tsx`\n  - `ArtifactInspection.tsx`\n  - artifact-only local scenes/tests\n\n- Quest surface writer:\n\n  - `SideQuestLedger.tsx`\n  - quest-only local scenes/tests\n  - PageFlip internals remain owned by the journal/Phase 2 lane\n\n- Log surface writer:\n\n  - `ShipsLog.tsx`\n  - log-only local scene/tests\n\n- Finale surface writer:\n\n  - `FinaleChamber.tsx`\n  - fallback semantic-state adapter/tests\n\n- Pause/resume/undo writer:\n\n  - preferably intentional new presentation components/tests after Phase 2 naming is known\n  - no edits to global host/queue files\n\n- Serialized integration ownership must remain with one coordinator for:\n\n  - `PlayerExperience.tsx`\n  - `TallTaleJournalSession.tsx`\n  - `story.scene.ts`\n  - `scene-registry.ts`\n  - `src/styles/player.css`\n  - shared domain/projection files\n\nSurface writers should hand CSS requirements to the single global-style owner or receive distinct verified family style files. They should not concurrently edit `player.css`.\n\n## Required stable-handle shape\n\nDiagnostic DOM attributes may expose identities, but Phase 2 external handles should be authoritative:\n\n- map: location key, route key, explicit route path, endpoint handles, fog-region handle;\n- artifacts: artifact key, exact destination slot, connection key/pair, source and endpoint handles;\n- quests: quest key plus objective ordinal;\n- annotation: annotation key plus visible page host;\n- log: progress-event ID on a Motion row wrapper with a separate GSAP ink child;\n- finale: requirement key and exact socket/path;\n- pause/resume/undo: immutable event ID plus state version and affected-object IDs.\n\n## Required tests\n\nFocused component/unit tests:\n\n- exact target selected by key when siblings and duplicates exist;\n- absent/off-section targets never satisfy local enhancement;\n- hidden PageFlip source and stale clone rejection;\n- local enhancement never acknowledges;\n- map changes only one marker/route/fog region;\n- artifact explicit source/destination and endpoint handoff;\n- Motion/GSAP wrapper separation for altar inspection and log rows;\n- quest discovery/update/completion target different exact elements;\n- quest page/index/filter preservation without unnecessary PageFlip recreation;\n- log newest-entry selection across multiple days, filters, long history, keyboard use, and route-back;\n- finale exact requirement update plus READY/COMPLETE distinction and fallback-state interface;\n- pause/resume controls, persistent state, state-version dedupe, no prior-scene replay;\n- undo exact previous/current summary and safe local reconciliation.\n\nIntegrated tests:\n\n- full 17 × 6 = 102 baseline matrix;\n- relevant-section local enhancement at most once;\n- all other sections remain mounted and receive only the global presentation;\n- canonical journal route, compatibility `/tale` route, and normalized adapters;\n- all five motion states;\n- six required viewports;\n- semantic visual checkpoints for map, artifact, quest, log, finale;\n- replay/refresh/interruption/reconnect;\n- no non-chapter false acknowledgment;\n- target cleanup and 20-cycle lifecycle baseline.\n\nCurrent coverage is insufficient: `workspace-interactions.test.tsx` covers navigation, objective expansion, and artifact focus only; `PlayerExperience.test.tsx` mocks every surface and tests chapter replay/failure only; `acceptance.spec.ts` checks eventual map/artifact state and offline pause/resume but not event-specific cinematic receipts. No 102-case matrix exists.\n\nConfidence is high for this static baseline and the missing-target/projection findings. No files, runtime, database, Git state, or validation resources were modified.\n\n## Gate and current authority\n\nPhase 3 cannot safely freeze its persistence design yet. The current tree has two distinct progression systems:\n\n| Surface | Business authority | Event authority | Presentation/replay authority |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Legacy `/tale/[campaignSlug]` | `Campaign.currentSequence` plus relational campaign state | `ProgressEvent`, unique `(campaignId, sequence)` | Only latest `CHAPTER_RELEASED` has persisted replay plus `ViewedCeremony` acknowledgment |\n| Canonical Tall Tale journal | Version-pinned `TaleSession`, `RevealState`, inventory, variables, verification state | `TaleSessionEvent`, unique `(sessionId, sequence)`, globally unique `idempotencyKey` | `PlayerJournalReadingState.lastEventSequence` only; no durable presentation receipt or replayable event history |\n\nThe Phase 3 uppercase 17-event taxonomy matches the legacy `ProgressEvent`/`PlayerExperience` surface, not the canonical Tall Tale event taxonomy. Phase 2 must declare which model is canonical or provide an explicit adapter. Re-read every path below after Phase 2 completes.\n\n## Exact current symbols\n\nPrimary legacy symbols:\n\n- `src/domain/story.ts`\n  - `eventTypes`, `ProgressEventType`\n  - `ReplayablePresentation`\n  - `PublicSnapshot`\n  - `ClientProgressEvent`\n  - `mergeEvents`\n- `src/domain/replay.ts`\n  - `StoredReplayEvent`\n  - `ReplayProjection`\n  - `projectChapterReleaseReplay`\n- `src/domain/visibility.ts`\n  - `publicKeys`\n  - `sanitizeEventPayload`\n  - `toClientEvent`\n- `src/lib/snapshot.ts`\n  - `buildPublicSnapshot`\n- `src/components/player/presentation-policy.ts`\n  - `decideChapterPresentation`\n  - `shouldSuppressChapterViewed`\n  - `toChapterReleaseClientEvent`\n  - `receiptValidatesAudio`\n- `src/components/player/PlayerExperience.tsx`\n  - `sceneByEvent`, `cueByEvent`\n  - `eventChain`, `seenEvents`, `latestSequence`\n  - `acknowledgedEvents`, `automaticPresentationAttempts`, `acknowledgmentRequests`\n  - `pendingMandatoryEventId`, `ceremonyGate`\n  - `playEvent`, `refreshSnapshot`, `acknowledgeCeremony`\n- `src/animation/director/AnimationDirector.ts`\n  - `play`, `execute`, `skip`, `cancel`\n  - `activeTerminal`, `activePriority`, FIFO `tail`\n  - `PresentationReceipt`\n- Routes:\n  - `src/app/api/player/[campaignSlug]/events/route.ts`\n  - `src/app/api/player/[campaignSlug]/snapshot/route.ts`\n  - `src/app/api/player/[campaignSlug]/viewed/route.ts`\n  - `src/app/api/player/[campaignSlug]/presence/route.ts`\n- Prisma:\n  - `Campaign`, `ProgressEvent`, `CampaignSnapshot`, `SaveStateSnapshot`\n  - `ViewedCeremony`, `ViewedContent`, `PlayerPresence`\n\nCanonical Tall Tale symbols:\n\n- `TaleSession`, `TaleSessionEvent`, `RevealState`, `PlaythroughMembership`\n- `appendEvent`, `emit`, `getTaleSessionState`, `captainControlTaleSession` in `src/tall-tale/progression.ts`\n- `TallTaleJournalSession`\n  - `processedSequence`, `pendingEvent`, `load`, `saveReading`\n- `PlayerJournalReadingState.lastEventSequence`\n- `getPlayerJournalReadingState`, `updatePlayerJournalReadingState`\n- `src/app/api/play/sessions/[sessionId]/events/route.ts`\n\n## Required invariants and test oracles\n\n| Case | Required authoritative source | Observable oracle | Current status |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Live event | Durable event row plus authoritative state version | One scene attempt in sequence order; valid final state; one eligible acknowledgment | Partial only |\n| Replay | Immutable Player-safe event envelope | New scene instance; identical safe payload; current motion policy; zero business/viewed writes | Latest chapter only |\n| Duplicate receipt | Immutable event ID plus payload identity | No scene/audio/ack/order change; one duplicate receipt recorded | Missing; silently discarded |\n| Out-of-order events | Durable sequence | Presentation order equals ascending sequence regardless arrival order | Missing |\n| Interrupted replay | Queue source/priority plus Director receipt | Replay normalizes, never acknowledges, live event wins, replay remains available | Unsafe |\n| Interrupted mandatory live scene | Persisted acknowledgment truth | No acknowledgment; retry survives remount; no detached work | Chapter release only |\n| Offline before event | Snapshot/event cursor | No false presentation or acknowledgment; reconnect catches the event | Partial |\n| Offline during scene | Event plus policy-declared interruption action | Exactly one terminal receipt; resume/restart/readable-final behavior | Not implemented above Director level |\n| Offline before acknowledgment | Idempotent persisted acknowledgment | Lost response reconciles through readback without duplicate presentation | Chapter release only |\n| Multiple missed events | Consistent snapshot plus Player-safe event range | One readable summary, then mandatory events ordered by sequence | Missing |\n| Stale event | Snapshot version and event sequence | No state regression or ceremony; explicit stale/duplicate receipt | Missing |\n| Revocation | Live authorization/membership truth | Queue/audio/retries stop; private state/cache cleared; terminal UI | Unsafe |\n| Pause | `Campaign.status`/`TaleSession.status` plus event version | Readable paused state persists; controls and ambient policy reconcile | Visual mapping exists; lifecycle incomplete |\n| Resume | Current status/version plus resume event | One transition for that version; no old ceremonies replay | Incomplete |\n| Undo | Current snapshot plus immutable revert event containing exact affected identities | Before/current summary, local reconciliation, truthful retained history | Payload insufficient |\n| One-shot | Durable receipt keyed by declared scope | Failure/abort does not consume; replay does not rewrite | Ad hoc/conflicting |\n| Acknowledgment | Separate presentation receipt, not transport/state cursor | Only valid live/reconnect terminal outcome writes once | Chapter release only |\n\n## Critical confirmed gaps and races\n\n1. Both SSE routes perform `findMany()` before registering `eventBus.on()`. An event emitted in that gap is lost until a later reconnect.\n\n2. `PlayerExperience` marks an event seen and advances `latestSequence` before presentation completes. A failed non-chapter event is therefore consumed across refresh without retry.\n\n3. Arrival order, not authoritative sequence, controls `eventChain`. Live SSE happens to send backlog ascending, but the client does not protect against out-of-order live delivery.\n\n4. `playEvent()` unconditionally aborts `presentationRun.current`. A user-started replay can abort an authoritative live scene, violating live-over-replay priority.\n\n5. The Director’s numeric priority can cancel an active lower-priority scene but cannot reorder its existing FIFO tail. A newly arrived critical scene can still sit behind previously queued medium work.\n\n6. Duplicate IDs are discarded before Director playback. No duplicate receipt or integrity-collision evidence is produced. Same ID with different type, sequence, or payload is not distinguished from a benign redelivery.\n\n7. Only chapter release consumes `decideChapterPresentation()` and persists `ViewedCeremony`. Other production scene contracts declare Player acknowledgment ownership, but no durable acknowledgment follows.\n\n8. `PlayerPresence.acknowledgedSequence` is populated from `snapshot.sequence`, not presentation receipts. GM diagnostics can report “synchronized” while a mandatory ceremony failed or remains unacknowledged.\n\n9. Current replay is not the immutable event payload required by Phase 3. `projectChapterReleaseReplay()` preserves event identity/title but composes narrative/objective/riddle from current readable chapter state.\n\n10. Replay history is limited to the latest chapter release. There is no all-event replay surface or replay after later releases.\n\n11. `TallTaleJournalSession.load(event)` changes an event sequence to `max(event.sequence, currentSequence)` while retaining its old event type. A quick pause/resume can announce “paused” at the resume sequence and then discard the actual resume event as stale.\n\n12. `pendingEvent` is a single slot. Concurrent `load()` calls can overwrite missed events rather than queue them.\n\n13. Tall Tale initialization sets `processedSequence` directly to current session sequence and persists it. Previously unseen events are consumed without a reconciliation summary or mandatory-scene queue.\n\n14. Journal reading state is stored inside the whole `PlayerProfile.preferences` JSON document. Concurrent tabs can read the same old value and overwrite each other; a page-only write can regress persisted `lastEventSequence`.\n\n15. Tall Tale pause/resume changes status before idempotency is checked in `appendEvent()`. Reusing an old pause key after a later resume can change status back to paused without creating a corresponding new event.\n\n16. Legacy undo exposes only `reversedType` to Players. It lacks exact affected identities and before/current values. The original event is not visibly superseded, so repeated undo selection can target stale history.\n\n17. Tall Tale SSE revocation closes the source, but the offline polling effect immediately continues `load()` every five seconds and the Retry button remains active.\n\n18. Legacy campaign SSE validates access only when opening. It has no periodic revocation check and can continue streaming after access removal.\n\n19. Revocation does not clear replay payloads, state, DOM, audio, local persistence, or queued work.\n\n20. Session-authorized media responses use `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable`. Private/revealed assets can survive revocation in browser or shared caches.\n\n21. Both event serializers expose event type, ID, sequence, and timing for event classes outside the Phase 3 Player allowlist. Tall Tale state also returns all recent event types/source types with sanitized payloads. Operational/private metadata remains inferable.\n\n## Recommended authority split\n\nPhase 3 should keep four truths separate:\n\n1. Business truth: one canonical progression aggregate and durable ordered event log.\n2. Player-safe immutable event truth: a versioned replay envelope created transactionally with the authoritative event.\n3. Presentation truth: one terminal controller receipt per accepted attempt.\n4. Acknowledgment truth: an idempotent server record keyed by Player/membership, event ID, and policy version.\n\nClient state should separately track:\n\n- last transport-received sequence;\n- last state-reconciled sequence;\n- pending/active/terminal presentation records;\n- last persisted presentation acknowledgment.\n\nNeither snapshot sequence nor reading position may masquerade as presentation acknowledgment.\n\nFor reconnect, retrieve an authorization-consistent snapshot, Player-safe events through its sequence, and acknowledgment statuses. Merge by ID, detect identity collisions, sort by sequence, show a bounded summary, then queue mandatory events. The SSE server must either register/buffer before reading backlog or perform a second catch-up read after listener registration.\n\nFor revocation:\n\n- abort Director/controller work;\n- clear the queue and retry timers;\n- stop audio and ambient work;\n- discard replay/state payloads and private DOM;\n- clear approved local/session caches;\n- render a terminal state;\n- make session/invitation media `private, no-store`;\n- never treat revocation as ordinary reconnecting.\n\n## One-shot scope recommendation\n\nThis remains a product-policy decision but should be explicit:\n\n- First journal introduction: currently conflicts—legacy is once per browser tab/campaign; Tall Tale is once per Player/playthrough.\n- Journal reentry: once per route entry, never a progression acknowledgment.\n- Automatic progression events: once per `(Player or membership, eventId, policyVersion)`.\n- Pause/resume: once per immutable transition event/state version.\n- Undo: once per `STATE_REVERTED` event.\n- Non-looping ink: once per presentation instance at its semantic label.\n- Section-local enhancement: once per event, section target, and authoritative state version.\n- Manual replay: unlimited policy-approved attempts; must never rewrite the automatic one-shot receipt.\n\nA failed, invisible, aborted, interrupted, timed-out, or ownership-rejected mandatory attempt must leave this key unconsumed.\n\n## Test protocol and isolation\n\nUnit tests:\n\n- Exhaustive `Record<PlayerProgressionEventType, Policy>` so a new Player event fails typecheck.\n- Pure queue reducer with fake clock: ordering, dedupe, collision, preemption, retry bounds, stale events, replay arbitration.\n- One-shot state machine and acknowledgment eligibility for every terminal outcome.\n- Pause/resume collapse and undo summaries.\n- Player-safe replay-envelope privacy sentinels.\n\nComponent tests:\n\n- Fake EventSource, fetch, Director, audio, focus, and visibility.\n- Every interruption in Phase 3 §39.\n- Duplicate ID produces no scene/audio/ack.\n- Replay cannot preempt live.\n- Live preempts replay only through a declared semantic boundary.\n- Remount/reconnect restores unacknowledged mandatory work.\n- Revocation proves zero retry timers, empty queue, cleared content, stopped audio, and terminal focus.\n- Concurrent reading-state writes require CAS/version or a dedicated row.\n\nIntegration tests:\n\n- Use a unique isolated database; never the shared canonical database.\n- Test backlog/listener race, `Last-Event-ID`, out-of-order batches, lost acknowledgment response, membership revocation, and idempotent pause/resume.\n- Assert exact event rows, acknowledgment rows, sequence values, and absence of mutation during replay.\n- Verify Player event allowlists against every stored event type.\n\nE2E:\n\n- Mutation workflows in serialized Chromium only; WebKit stays read-only.\n- One authoritative server/database/browser owner.\n- For every replayable scene apply the exact 12-step §37 protocol.\n- Capture all network requests and database before/after state; replay may issue only read-only snapshot/history requests.\n- Exercise browser offline mode before event, mid-scene, and after presentation/before acknowledgment.\n- Inject duplicate, stale, and multi-event reconnect batches.\n- Revoke membership during active presentation and prove terminal cleanup plus uncached private assets.\n- Verify zero leaked Director scene, ownership claim, audio instance, timeout, EventSource, or detached DOM write after each interruption.\n\n## Confidence and handoff\n\n- High confidence: current schemas, event routes, replay/acknowledgment paths, revocation behavior, cache policy, and identified code races.\n- Medium confidence: final Phase 3 persistence shape, because Phase 2’s host/controller interfaces and canonical event bridge are not yet present.\n- Required before any Phase 3 edit: reread all listed files, the completed Phase 2 design/implementation/validation reports, current schema/migrations, and the working-tree diff.\n\n"}},{"agent_name":"/root/p3_r7_a11y_quality","agent_status":"running"},{"agent_name":"/root/p3_r8_validation","agent_status":{"completed":"P3-R8 complete — read-only validation/isolation warmup. There are currently 46 Vitest files.\n- [playwright.config.ts](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/playwright.config.ts) is deliberately serialized: `workers: 1`, `fullyParallel: false`, Chromium desktop plus iPhone-14 WebKit, port `3100`, no server reuse. There are nine E2E files and zero Phase 3 matrix files.\n- [scripts/test-all.ps1](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/scripts/test-all.ps1) and [prepare-validation-isolation.ts](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/scripts/prepare-validation-isolation.ts) provide a strong fail-closed integrated isolation gate:\n  - three stable fingerprints of canonical `dev.db`, WAL, SHM, and journal;\n  - uniquely named copied database under the validation runtime;\n  - explicit absolute `DATABASE_URL`;\n  - nonce marker written only into the copy;\n  - application-level nonce query;\n  - launcher/listener PID, creation time, process ancestry, and port ownership;\n  - pre-browser mutation checkpoint;\n  - isolated-copy mutation and canonical-family invariance verification;\n  - owned-process cleanup and port-release checks.\n- The only currently safe mutation-capable browser command is the coordinator-owned `npm run validate`. Direct `npm run test:e2e` does not itself prepare the copied database and therefore must not be used for Phase 3 mutation testing.\n- Existing reusable proof:\n  - [lanternwake-phase1.spec.ts](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/tests/e2e/lanternwake-phase1.spec.ts): required-target failure, acknowledgment gating, refresh replay, unsafe-request monitoring, broad persisted-state comparison.\n  - [lanternwake-journal.spec.ts](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/tests/e2e/lanternwake-journal.spec.ts): listener/EventSource/fetch accounting and `<250 ms` unmount cleanup.\n  - [lanternwake-access-transitions.spec.ts](C:/Users/kkids/Documents/Codex_TreasureHunt/tests/e2e/lanternwake-access-transitions.spec.ts): committed-pose/no-snapback proof.\n  - Target, ownership, Director, motion-provider, PageFlip, Lottie, Rive, audio, replay, and telemetry unit tests already supply useful primitives.\n\n## Required Phase 3 test structure\n\nIntentional new E2E files:\n\n- `tests/e2e/support/lanternwake-phase3.ts` — exhaustive event/section constants, isolated fixtures, telemetry/network/database oracles.\n- `tests/e2e/lanternwake-phase3-matrix.spec.ts` — 102 full-mode cases at `1440x900`.\n- `tests/e2e/lanternwake-phase3-modes.spec.ts` — literal 205 runs: 17 relevant-section events × 5 modes, plus four global events × 6 sections × 5 modes.\n- `tests/e2e/lanternwake-phase3-viewports.spec.ts` — all frozen P0/P1 flows at six exact viewports; currently at least 13 named flows × 6 = 78.\n- `tests/e2e/lanternwake-phase3-replay-one-shot.spec.ts` — 12-step replay protocol plus 14 one-shot surfaces.\n- `tests/e2e/lanternwake-phase3-interruptions-fallback.spec.ts` — 13 interruption classes and 15 forced failures.\n- `tests/e2e/lanternwake-phase3-accessibility.spec.ts` — keyboard, focus, live regions, Axe, hidden PageFlip source, sound independence.\n- `tests/e2e/lanternwake-phase3-lifecycle.spec.ts` — twenty-cycle host/runtime/listener stress.\n- `tests/e2e/lanternwake-phase3-production-performance.spec.ts` — production-build measurements only.\n\nEach 102-cell test should:\n\n1. Require `/api/dev/validation/database-identity` before any mutation.\n2. Create a fresh authoritative fixture and unique event ID/sequence.\n3. Start in the named section and retain the original focus target.\n4. Trigger the real server/domain command, not a client-only event.\n5. Wait on semantic telemetry/checkpoints, never arbitrary sleeps.\n6. Assert scene, host, scene instance, target/visibility/duplicate counts, ownership, PageFlip-source rejection, semantic labels, final state, section preservation, focus restoration, local-enhancement policy, snapshot sequence, receipt order, one allowed viewed receipt, replay availability, and cleanup.\n7. Attach one bounded JSON result per cell so the telemetry recorder’s current 100-entry in-memory capacity cannot discard evidence from a 102-case run.\n\n## Isolation and ownership\n\nOne exclusive runtime/database/Playwright owner must control:\n\n- copied database and fixture mutations;\n- Chromium mutation cases;\n- ports `3100` and `3200`;\n- browser/server lifecycle;\n- production build and performance run;\n- final E2E and `npm run validate`.\n\nWebKit remains read-only. Its specs should fail immediately if any non-GET/HEAD API request occurs. No worker acquires port `3000`.\n\nBefore enabling focused browser execution, add a safe isolated runner or a narrowly scoped option to `test-all.ps1` that performs the same copy/nonce/PID/checkpoint/final-verification sequence. Until then, only focused Vitest/component commands are safe to parallelize.\n\nThe isolation report should additionally record per-fixture deltas for viewed ceremonies/content, snapshots, event IDs/sequences, acknowledgments and relevant audit records; the current global helper counts only five model families and is insufficient as the complete 102-cell receipt oracle.\n\n## Execution order\n\n1. Parallel focused contract/policy, queue/host, scene-runtime, section-component, replay, and accessibility Vitest shards.\n2. Integrated format, lint, typecheck, unit and asset gates.\n3. Exclusive isolation preflight.\n4. Serialized Chromium 102-case matrix.\n5. Serialized mode, viewport, replay, interruption, fallback and accessibility specs.\n6. Twenty-cycle lifecycle run.\n7. One production build, then performance/leak measurement during the first port-3200 start and restart proof during the second.\n8. One final `npm run validate`.\n\n## Gaps and blockers\n\n- Phase 1 browser/integrated validation is still documented as pending; Phase 3 must not treat its static specifications as runtime passes.\n- Phase 2 is actively changing the working tree. Re-read the relevant implementation, tests, configs, and isolation scripts after Phase 2 completes and before any Phase 3 edit or run.\n- No persistent `ProgressionSceneHost`, exhaustive Phase 3 policy test, 102-case fixture, or Phase 3 E2E file exists yet.\n- Current production scene contracts use inconsistent host kinds (`player-progression` for chapter release versus `progression` for several others).\n- P0/P1 classification is not frozen beyond chapter release being P0, so the final viewport count cannot yet be closed.\n- Current browser-accessible metrics do not count SceneHosts, ownership claims, timers, all listeners, or detached nodes.\n- Production performance/leak execution is not integrated into the current build/restart workflow.\n- Real production Rive artwork remains an external/missing-Phase-5-asset classification; fallback proof cannot be reported as Rive readiness.\n\nConfidence: high for the static capability and safety assessment; medium for final case counts beyond the mandated matrices because Phase 2 and the Phase 3 policy/replay/P0-P1 intake are still moving.\n\n"}}]}

## Tool Call: exec
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-18T21:59:43.188Z

  threadId: "019f771a-30e8-7cc2-9dfd-87180f1919e6",
  hostId: "local",
  turnLimit: 1,
  includeOutputs: false
});

const items = obj.turns?.[0]?.items ?? [];
const msgs = items.filter(x => x.type === "agentMessage").slice(-12).map(x => ({id:x.id, phase:x.phase, text:x.text}));
text(msgs);

That means a production builder cannot animate its approved targets without falling back to raw DOM queries.\\n- **Integration state:** C1 output is reviewed but not accepted as complete. S2 is continuing the non-blocked 28-scene registry/tombstone work and is prohibited from converting production builders until the API is repaired.\\n- **Recovery:** C1 is reopened as C1b with the same exclusive core-file ownership to add the missing immutable permit-gated adapter/context path and regression tests. No component writer owns those files, so there is no collision. The fix unblocks S2 without discarding any completed work.\\n\\nThis is exactly the kind of boundary defect the independent migration lane is meant to catch; it was found before any raw-query migration could be falsely accepted.\"}]}", "type": "input_text"}]

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-18T23:17:07.529Z

Independent review caught a real Phase 2 boundary defect before acceptance: the Director acquired atomic permits but discarded them before `SceneBuildContext`, leaving builders unable to animate approved elements without raw queries. The 19-file core delivery and 92/92 tests are preserved, but core is reopened for a permit-gated adapter/context path and regressions. Scene registry/tombstone work can continue, while production builder conversion remains correctly blocked.

S2 owns scene definitions; integration follows | Parallelizes the route-transition and command-center risk |\\n\\nSix workers launched; with C1 still finishing its bounded verification, seven direct workers are active and six direct slots remain available. The coordinator owns the original requirements, dependency graph, file-conflict prevention, reconciliation acceptance, shared server/ports/browser/database, manifests/lockfiles, styles and parent integration, production build/E2E/full validation, Git, final documentation/synchronization, and the final response. The PlayerExperience/style integration lane and independent cross-cutting verification are queued behind these non-overlapping writers. No worker may control port 3000, install dependencies, run full validation, or modify shared Git state.\"},{\"id\":\"item-120\",\"text\":\"Core completion review found one material integration gap before acceptance:\\n\\n- **C1 worker complete:** 19-file runtime delivered with `92/92` focused tests, typecheck, lint, formatting, and diff checks green; its slot was released.\\n- **Independent S2 finding:** The Director acquires atomic write permits but currently discards them before `SceneBuildContext`; builders receive receipt IDs, not permit-bound element adapters. That means a production builder cannot animate its approved targets without falling back to raw DOM queries.\\n- **Integration state:** C1 output is reviewed but not accepted as complete. S2 is continuing the non-blocked 28-scene registry/tombstone work and is prohibited from converting production builders until the API is repaired.\\n- **Recovery:** C1 is reopened as C1b with the same exclusive core-file ownership to add the missing immutable permit-gated adapter/context path and regression tests. No component writer owns those files, so there is no collision. The fix unblocks S2 without discarding any completed work.\\n\\nThis is exactly the kind of boundary defect the independent migration lane is meant to catch; it was found before any raw-query migration could be falsely accepted.\"}]}", "type": "input_text"}]

Use timeoutMs: 0 for an immediate snapshot. Commentary never wakes the wait. An up-to-date cursor omits previously delivered final text; a timeout includes compact progress for all targets. Per-target failures are returned in errors.\\n\\nexec tool declaration:\\n```ts\\ndeclare const tools: { codex_app__wait_threads(args: {\\n  // Threads to wait for. The first target that completes or needs attention wins.\\n  targets: Array<{\\n  // Optional cursor returned by an earlier wait.\\n  afterCursor?: string;\\n  // Optional host id returned by create_thread or list_threads.\\n  hostId?: string;\\n  // Thread id to wait for.\\n  threadId: string;\\n}>;\\n  // Maximum event-wait time in milliseconds. A bounded snapshot fetch for fresh progress may add latency. Defaults to 120000.\\n  timeoutMs?: number;\\n}): Promise<unknown>; };\\n```\"}]", "type": "input_text"}]

production builders consume immutable handles with no raw query escape | No core runtime, components, styles, docs, runtime, Git | Uses C1 API; I review registry counts, selectors, and tests | Removes the largest production-compatibility block |\\n| P2 | PageFlip boundary | Active | Write | Existing `PageFlipBook`, showcase, physical journal, side-quest ledger and tests; new PlayerSignIn test | Instance-local command/access hosts, semantic Motion ownership, success-hold/failure restoration contracts | No access/command scene builders, APIs, styles, parent, runtime, docs | Uses C1 API; That means a production builder cannot animate its approved targets without falling back to raw DOM queries.\\n- **Integration state:** C1 output is reviewed but not accepted as complete. S2 is continuing the non-blocked 28-scene registry/tombstone work and is prohibited from converting production builders until the API is repaired.\\n- **Recovery:** C1 is reopened as C1b with the same exclusive core-file ownership to add the missing immutable permit-gated adapter/context path and regression tests. No component writer owns those files, so there is no collision. The fix unblocks S2 without discarding any completed work.\\n\\nThis is exactly the kind of boundary defect the independent migration lane is meant to catch; it was found before any raw-query migration could be falsely accepted.\",\"phase\":\"commentary\"},{\"type\":\"subAgentActivity\",\"id\":\"call_SX8ORMbIj8kztzZ1gmTkxf73\",\"kind\":\"interacted\",\"agentThreadId\":\"019f7770-6893-7363-b939-6a7ae1997e56\",\"agentPath\":\"/root/c1_core_runtime\"},{\"type\":\"subAgentActivity\",\"id\":\"call_ymSzYsFrk9tfJtya33QX1NWx\",\"kind\":\"interacted\",\"agentThreadId\":\"019f7782-a599-7970-8277-60542e3828a6\",\"agentPath\":\"/root/s2_scene_migration\"},{\"type\":\"subAgentActivity\",\"id\":\"call_OByobQEioMhwaktyYSUurLWm\",\"kind\":\"interacted\",\"agentThreadId\":\"019f7782-a599-7970-8277-60542e3828a6\",\"agentPath\":\"/root/s2_scene_migration\"},{\"type\":\"subAgentActivity\",\"id\":\"call_Qq8OKd5mud1szxc8bWqPgqaO\",\"kind\":\"interacted\",\"agentThreadId\":\"019f7784-4bc0-7a93-bb85-fa4e71cefd70\",\"agentPath\":\"/root/a2_access_quartermaster\"},{\"type\":\"agentMessage\",\"id\":\"item-121\",\"text\":\"A second API integration issue surfaced cleanly: Chart route targets are `SVGPathElement`, but the new registration types accept only `HTMLElement`. The runtime logic is otherwise element-generic.\\n\\nC2 remains inside its files and may use a temporary bounded cast to keep progress moving. C1b now also owns the type correction to `Element`/SVG-safe targets plus regression coverage; C2 will remove the cast after that handoff. No ownership overlap or delay to the other lanes.\",\"phase\":\"commentary\"},{\"type\":\"subAgentActivity\",\"id\":\"call_omGDd7oVvuKYdfeZUX7Az5CR\",\"kind\":\"interacted\",\"agentThreadId\":\"019f7770-6893-7363-b939-6a7ae1997e56\",\"agentPath\":\"/root/c1_core_runtime\"}]}]}", "type": "input_text"}]
